---
title: "ORF-Centric Hybrid Model for NMD Prediction"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide
    fig_width: 8
    fig_height: 5
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(jsonlite)
library(DT)
library(pROC)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(cluster)
library(ggseqlogo)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Publication theme and color palette
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
theme_pub <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
theme_set(theme_pub)

pal <- c(NMD = "#ef8a62", Control = "#67a9cf")
nuc_colors <- c(A = "#109648", C = "#255C99", G = "#F7B32B", T = "#D62839")
pal_type <- c(`ORF structural` = "#d95f02", Sequence = "#7570b3")
ejc_pal <- c("EJC 1" = "#e41a1c", "EJC 2" = "#377eb8", "EJC 3" = "#4daf4a",
             "EJC 4" = "#984ea3", "EJC 5+" = "#ff7f00")
subgroup_pal_short <- c("NMD PTC+" = "#ef8a62",
                         "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained" = "#fdd49e",
                         "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost" = "#fdbb84",
                         "Control" = "#67a9cf")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
render_dt <- function(df, caption = NULL, pageLength = 15, ...) {
  datatable(df, caption = caption, rownames = FALSE,
            options = list(pageLength = pageLength, scrollX = TRUE), ...)
}

fig_dir <- "figures"
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

save_fig <- function(p, name, width = 8, height = 5) {
  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0(name, ".pdf")), p,
         width = width, height = height)
  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0(name, ".png")), p,
         width = width, height = height, dpi = 300)
  print(p)
}

results_dir <- "results"
```

# 0. Data Loading and Model Overview {#sec0-data}

```{r best-model-params}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Best model: ATG=500, STOP=500 (selected by AUPRC across window sweep)
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Model Training and Window Size Sweep"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
best_atg  <- 500
best_stop <- 500
best_tag  <- paste0("atg", best_atg, "_stop", best_stop)
cat("Best model:", best_tag, "\n")
```

```{r load-all-metrics}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: metrics_atg{A}_stop{S}.json — produced by evaluate.py
# Population: 12 models (3 ATG × 4 STOP window sizes)

atg_sizes  <- c(100, 500, 1000)
stop_sizes <- c(100, 500, 1000, 2000)

metrics_list <- list()
for (a in atg_sizes) {
  for (s in stop_sizes) {
    tag <- paste0("atg", a, "_stop", s)
    path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("metrics_", tag, ".json"))
    if (file.exists(path)) {
      m <- fromJSON(path)
      metrics_list[[tag]] <- data.frame(
        tag = tag, atg_window = a, stop_window = s,
        auc = m$auc, auprc = m$auprc,
        n_test = m$n_test, n_nmd = m$n_nmd,
        best_epoch = m$best_epoch,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
}
all_metrics <- bind_rows(metrics_list) %>% arrange(desc(auprc))
```

```{r load-best-model}
# ── Data provenance ──
# All files below are for the best model (ATG=500, STOP=500).
# Population: Test set = chr 1, 3, 5, 7 (paralog-free holdout).
# Training/validation used remaining chromosomes.

# Predictions (one row per test transcript)
preds <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("predictions_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# Add outcome classification (used by multiple sections downstream)
preds <- preds %>%
  mutate(predicted = ifelse(prob > 0.5, 1, 0),
         outcome = case_when(
           label == 1 & predicted == 1 ~ "TP",
           label == 1 & predicted == 0 ~ "FN",
           label == 0 & predicted == 1 ~ "FP",
           label == 0 & predicted == 0 ~ "TN"
         ))

# Attention weights (one row per ORF per transcript)
attn_weights <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                      paste0("attention_weights_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# Attention entropy (one row per transcript)
attn_entropy <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                      paste0("attention_entropy_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# Attention by ORF type (summary statistics)
attn_by_type <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                      paste0("attention_by_orf_type_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# Attention vs ORF feature correlations
attn_vs_feat <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                      paste0("attention_vs_features_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# v5: ORF structural feature importance only (no TX bypass)
imp_orf <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                 paste0("structural_importance_orf_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
imp_rank <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                  paste0("structural_importance_by_rank_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# No-PTC NMD analysis (all NMD test transcripts with ORF-level detail)
noptc_all <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                   paste0("attention_noptc_analysis_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
noptc <- noptc_all %>% filter(any_orf_has_ejc == 0)

# Selected ORFs (full dataset — filtered to test set below)
# Source: data_prep.py::select_priority_orfs(); see METHODS.md § "v2 Priority ORF Selection"
all_orfs <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "selected_orfs.tsv"))

# Reference CDS features (for 3'UTR correction analysis in Section 7)
# Source: export_rds.R from 05t_ref_cds_features.R; see METHODS.md § "Transcript-level features"
ref_features <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "ref_cds_features.tsv"))

# TD2 features (needed for subgroup assignment in Sections 5.1 and 7)
td2_features <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "td2_features.tsv"))

# DeepSHAP channel summary (mean across 5 replicates)
# Source: deepshap.py; see METHODS.md § "DeepSHAP Sequence Interpretation"
# Numeric cols: mean_abs_shap, mean_abs_shap_nmd, mean_abs_shap_ctrl (safe to average)
shap_run_files <- sort(Sys.glob(file.path(results_dir,
                                           paste0("deepshap_summary_", best_tag, "_run*.tsv"))))
has_shap <- length(shap_run_files) > 0
if (has_shap) {
  shap_runs <- lapply(shap_run_files, read.delim)
  shap_summary <- bind_rows(shap_runs) %>%
    group_by(branch, channel) %>%
    summarise(across(where(is.numeric), mean), .groups = "drop")
  cat(sprintf("DeepSHAP channel summary: mean of %d replicates\n", length(shap_run_files)))
}

# DeepSHAP positional TSVs (from run 1, exported by 06_export_deepshap_tsv.py)
shap_run_tag <- paste0(best_tag, "_run1")
shap_atg_pos_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("deepshap_atg_positional_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))
has_shap_tsv <- file.exists(shap_atg_pos_path)
if (has_shap_tsv) {
  shap_atg_pos   <- read.delim(shap_atg_pos_path)
  shap_stop_reg  <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                          paste0("deepshap_stop_regional_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv")))
  shap_stop_near <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                          paste0("deepshap_stop_near_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv")))
  shap_junction  <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                          paste0("deepshap_junction_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv")))
  shap_stop_ctx  <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir,
                                          paste0("deepshap_stop_codon_context_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv")))
}

# Motif landmark logos (from 07_motif_analysis.py)
# Prefer joint SHAP logos (all test samples) if available; fall back to marginal run 1
motif_atg_joint_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logo_atg_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
motif_stop_joint_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logo_stop_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
motif_stop_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logos_stop_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))
motif_atg_path  <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logo_atg_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))

if (file.exists(motif_atg_joint_path) && file.exists(motif_stop_joint_path)) {
  motif_atg  <- read.delim(motif_atg_joint_path)
  motif_stop <- read.delim(motif_stop_joint_path)
  has_motif_logos <- TRUE
  cat("Using joint SHAP motif logos (all test samples)\n")
} else if (file.exists(motif_stop_path) && file.exists(motif_atg_path)) {
  motif_stop <- read.delim(motif_stop_path)
  motif_atg  <- read.delim(motif_atg_path)
  has_motif_logos <- TRUE
  cat("Using marginal SHAP motif logos (run 1 subsample)\n")
} else {
  has_motif_logos <- FALSE
}
if (has_motif_logos) {
}

# Additional analysis TSVs
gc_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("gc_content_across_stop_window_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_gc <- file.exists(gc_path)
if (has_gc) gc_data <- read.delim(gc_path)

# v5: stop codon stats come from subgroup-level DeepSHAP (loaded as sg_stop_cd above)

pa_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("polya_signal_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))
has_pa <- file.exists(pa_path)
if (has_pa) pa_data <- read.delim(pa_path)

junc_ord_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("junction_ordinal_summary_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))
junc_raw_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("junction_by_ordinal_", shap_run_tag, ".tsv"))
has_junc_ord <- file.exists(junc_ord_path) && file.exists(junc_raw_path)
if (has_junc_ord) {
  junc_ord_summary <- read.delim(junc_ord_path)
  junc_raw <- read.delim(junc_raw_path)
}

# DeepSHAP stability
shap_stab_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("deepshap_stability_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_stability <- file.exists(shap_stab_path)
if (has_stability) shap_stability <- read.delim(shap_stab_path)

# Subgroup-specific DeepSHAP TSVs (from 08_export_subgroup_deepshap_tsv.py)
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Subgroup-Specific DeepSHAP Analysis"
sg_kozak_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_kozak_shap_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_subgroup_shap <- file.exists(sg_kozak_path)
if (has_subgroup_shap) {
  sg_kozak   <- read.delim(sg_kozak_path)
  sg_utr5    <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_utr5_channel_shap_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
  sg_stop_cd <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_stop_codon_shap_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
  sg_junc    <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_junction_downstream_shap_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
  sg_frame   <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_frame_periodicity_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
  sg_gc_ch   <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("subgroup_gc_channel_", best_tag, ".tsv")))
  cat("Subgroup-specific DeepSHAP TSVs loaded (6 files)\n")
}
```

```{r define-test-population}
# ── Test set population (consistent across all sections) ──
test_orfs <- all_orfs %>%
  semi_join(preds, by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, label, prob), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  mutate(nmd_status = ifelse(label == 1, "NMD", "Control"))

test_rank0 <- test_orfs %>% filter(orf_rank == 0)

n_test  <- nrow(preds)
n_nmd   <- sum(preds$label == 1)
n_ctrl  <- sum(preds$label == 0)
nmd_pct <- round(100 * n_nmd / n_test, 1)

cat(sprintf("Test population: %s transcripts (%s NMD [%s%%], %s Control)\n",
            format(n_test, big.mark = ","),
            format(n_nmd, big.mark = ","), nmd_pct,
            format(n_ctrl, big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("ORF-level rows (test): %s (up to 5 per transcript)\n",
            format(nrow(test_orfs), big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Holdout chromosomes: 1, 3, 5, 7 (never used for training or feature selection)\n"))
shap_n_nmd <- round(2000 * n_nmd / n_test)
cat(sprintf("DeepSHAP subset: 2,000 samples (~%d NMD, ~%d Control) per replicate\n",
            shap_n_nmd, 2000 - shap_n_nmd))
```

## Architecture and Interpretation Framework

```{r fig0-architecture, fig.width=16, fig.height=20, eval=file.exists("make_architecture_figure.R")}
# ── Source: make_architecture_figure.R (model architecture) ──
source("make_architecture_figure.R")
print(p)
```

```{r fig0-shap-interpretation, fig.width=12, fig.height=6, eval=file.exists("make_shap_interpretation_figure.R")}
# ── Source: make_shap_interpretation_figure.R (SHAP interpretation detail) ──
source("make_shap_interpretation_figure.R")
print(p_shap)
```

### From Transcript to Model Input

The model's input is derived from full-length spliced transcript sequences (SQANTI-corrected FASTA). For each transcript, ORFik scans all open reading frames and a priority selection algorithm chooses up to K=5 ORFs:

- **Rank 0:** The ORF whose ATG matches the **reference CDS** — the reading frame of the gene's dominant non-NMD isoform. Available for ~73% of transcripts; when unavailable, this slot is filled by the next priority tier.
- **Rank 1:** The ORF matching the **SQANTI/TransDecoder2 CDS** call (longest ORF with homology support), if different from the reference CDS.
- **Ranks 2–4:** Remaining slots filled by ORFs with the highest **Kozak consensus scores**. Ties in Kozak score are broken by transcript position (5'-most ORF wins), reflecting the ORFik scan order preserved by pandas stable sort.

For each selected ORF, the model receives three inputs:

**Two sequence windows** — one centered on the ATG (start codon) and one centered on the stop codon. Each window is 500bp total (±250bp around the middle nucleotide of the codon). Positions outside the transcript are zero-padded. Each position is encoded with 9 channels:

| Channel | Name | Type | Description |
|---------|------|------|-------------|
| 0–3 | A, C, G, T | Binary (one-hot) | Nucleotide identity; exactly one active per position |
| 4 | Exon-exon junction | Binary | 1 at splice junction positions within the transcript |
| 5 | Rolling GC | Continuous [0, 1] | GC fraction in a 50bp sliding window |
| 6–8 | Frame 0, 1, 2 | Binary (one-hot) | Codon position relative to this ORF's ATG |

**Five structural features** per ORF, capturing positional and annotation-level information:

| Feature | Type | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| frac_start | Continuous [0, 1] | Fractional position of the ORF start within the transcript |
| frac_stop | Continuous [0, 1] | Fractional position of the ORF stop within the transcript |
| is_ref_cds | Binary | 1 if this ORF's ATG matches the reference CDS |
| is_sqanti_cds | Binary | 1 if this ORF's ATG matches the SQANTI/TD2 CDS |
| n_downstream_ejc | Integer | Exon-exon junctions downstream of this ORF's stop codon |

The downstream EJC count is included as a structural feature because junctions beyond the 500bp stop-codon window boundary are invisible to the sequence CNN. This feature ensures the model has access to the classical NMD trigger (a premature termination codon with downstream exon-exon junctions) regardless of window size. For complete feature definitions, see METHODS.md § "Feature Definitions and CDS Sources."

### Three Levels of the Model

The model processes each transcript through three stages (levels 1–3 in the figure):

1. **Per-ORF encoding** (level 1, shared weights): Each ORF is independently processed by three parallel branches that share weights across all K ORFs:
    - **ATG CNN:** 9-channel × 500-position sequence window → two Conv1d layers (32 filters each) → adaptive max-pool → **32-dim embedding**
    - **Stop CNN:** Same architecture applied to the stop-codon window → **32-dim embedding**
    - **Structural FC:** 5 features → Linear + ReLU → **32-dim embedding**

    The three 32-dim embeddings are concatenated (96-dim) and passed through a fusion layer (Linear 96→64 + ReLU + Dropout) to produce a single **64-dim ORF embedding**. *Biological question: What does the sequence and structure around each ORF look like?*

2. **Attention aggregation** (level 2): A learned scoring function (Linear 64→1) assigns a scalar score to each ORF embedding. Scores are passed through a masked softmax (padding ORFs receive −∞) to produce attention weights that sum to 1 across valid ORFs. The transcript-level embedding is the weighted sum of ORF embeddings (64-dim). The attention weights are directly interpretable: an ORF with weight 0.85 contributes 85% of the information flowing to the classifier. *Biological question: Which candidate reading frame is most relevant for the NMD decision?*

3. **Classification** (level 3): The 64-dim transcript embedding passes through a classification head (Linear 64→32 + ReLU + Dropout → Linear 32→1) to produce a single NMD logit. There are no transcript-level bypass features — all information flows through the per-ORF encoders and attention mechanism. *Biological question: Given the attention-selected ORF representation, is this an NMD target?*

### Four Interpretation Methods at Three Model Layers

We use four complementary methods, each operating at a different layer of the model (annotated with dashed brackets in the figure):

1. **Joint DeepSHAP** (blue bracket, input layer): Varies all rank-0 ORF inputs simultaneously — both sequence windows and structural features — as a single 9,005-dimensional vector (9×500 + 9×500 + 5). Computes per-position, per-channel attributions through the full model to the output logit. Background: 500 training samples; 5 independent replicates for variance estimation. Attributions are additive: the sum of all 9,005 SHAP values equals f(x) − E[f(x)]. *Used in: Sections 2.2–2.3, 3, 4, 6.3–6.6.*

2. **KernelSHAP — branch decomposition** (red bracket, fusion layer): Treats the three 32-dim sub-embeddings (ATG CNN output, stop CNN output, structural FC output) as three "players" in a cooperative game. Evaluates all 2³ = 8 coalitions of present/absent branches, integrating over background embeddings for absent branches. Produces exact Shapley values with zero residual. This gives the coarse branch-level attribution (e.g., structural 61%, stop 29%, ATG 10% for NMD transcripts). *Used in: Sections 2.1, 6.7.*

3. **DeepSHAP — structural features** (structural slice of Joint DeepSHAP): The last 5 values of the 9,005-dim joint attribution vector, corresponding to the structural features. These tell us which structural properties (e.g., downstream EJC count, CDS identity) shift the prediction and in which direction. *Used in: Sections 2.2, 6.6.*

4. **Attention weights** (purple label, aggregator): The softmax-normalized scores over K ORFs, summing to 1 per transcript. Deterministic given the input — no stochastic background sampling. *Used in: Sections 5, 6.8.*

**How they relate:** KernelSHAP at the fusion layer provides the coarse branch decomposition — how much each information stream (ATG sequence, stop sequence, structural features) contributes. Joint DeepSHAP at the input layer provides fine-grained per-position attributions within each branch. Attention weights at the aggregator reveal which ORF the model selects. Together: *which branch matters most → which positions/features within that branch → which ORF.*

**What they cannot tell us:** (1) Interaction effects between branches (e.g., whether Kozak strength modulates the EJC signal) — each method conditions on one layer. (2) Codon-level dependencies — per-nucleotide SHAP captures positional composition, not triplet identity. (3) Causal relationships — all metrics measure association with the model's output, not biological causation.

**Note on edge artifacts:** CNN-based DeepSHAP values can be elevated at the boundaries of sequence windows due to the CNN's receptive field extending beyond the window edge. The SHAP profile plots in Section 2.3 show modest elevation at both edges of the stop window (positions ±230–250), consistent with a boundary effect. The first and last ~25bp of each window should be interpreted with this caveat.

---

# 1. Model Performance Comparison {#sec1-performance}

**Question:** Which combination of ATG and stop codon window sizes yields the best NMD prediction?

**Population:** `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts (`r format(n_nmd, big.mark = ",")` NMD, `r nmd_pct`% prevalence). Test set is identical across all models. Methods: see METHODS.md § "Model Training and Window Size Sweep."

```{r fig1a-auprc-heatmap, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: metrics_atg{A}_stop{S}.json — produced by evaluate.py
# Population: 12 models tested on the same holdout set

p_auprc <- all_metrics %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(stop_window), y = factor(atg_window), fill = auprc)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", auprc)), size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#67a9cf", mid = "#f7f7f7", high = "#ef8a62",
                       midpoint = median(all_metrics$auprc), name = "Test AUPRC") +
  labs(title = sprintf("Test AUPRC by Window Size (3x4 sweep, n=%s)", format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = sprintf("AUPRC: area under precision-recall curve. Higher = better. %s NMD (%s%% prevalence).",
                          format(n_nmd, big.mark = ","), nmd_pct),
       x = "Stop codon window (bp)", y = "ATG window (bp)") +
  coord_equal()

save_fig(p_auprc, "fig1a_auprc_heatmap", width = 7, height = 5)
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **ATG window:** Sequence context (bp) extracted around each ORF's start codon, encoded with 9 channels (4 nucleotide one-hot + splice junction + rolling GC + 3 reading-frame one-hot).
- **Stop window:** Same 9-channel encoding around each ORF's stop codon.
- **AUPRC:** Area under the precision-recall curve. More informative than AUC for imbalanced classes (NMD prevalence = `r nmd_pct`%).

```{r fig1b-extended-sweep, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: metrics_atg500_stop{S}.json — produced by evaluate.py
# Population: 4 ATG=500 models on same holdout set
ext_metrics <- all_metrics %>%
  filter(atg_window == 500) %>%
  arrange(stop_window)

ext_long <- ext_metrics %>%
  select(stop_window, auc, auprc) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(auc, auprc), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = toupper(metric))

p_ext <- ext_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(stop_window), y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", value)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(AUC = "#67a9cf", AUPRC = "#ef8a62")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.02)) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Performance vs Stop Window Size (ATG=500 fixed, n=%s)", format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "STOP=500 is optimal by AUPRC. Performance declines at larger stop windows.",
       x = "Stop codon window (bp)", y = "Performance", fill = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_ext, "fig1b_extended_sweep", width = 8, height = 5)
```

<details><summary>Table 1: All model metrics</summary>

```{r table1}
# Source: all_metrics (loaded in Section 0 from evaluate.py JSON files)
render_dt(all_metrics %>%
            mutate(auc = round(auc, 4), auprc = round(auprc, 4)) %>%
            select(tag, atg_window, stop_window, auc, auprc, best_epoch, n_test, n_nmd),
          caption = "Table 1: All models ranked by AUPRC")
```
</details>

```{r fig1c-training-curves, fig.height=10}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: training_log_{best_tag}.csv — epoch-level train/val metrics from 03_train.py

log_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("training_log_", best_tag, ".csv"))
if (file.exists(log_path)) {
  log_df <- read.csv(log_path)

  p1 <- ggplot(log_df, aes(x = epoch)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = train_auc, color = "Train"), linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_line(aes(y = val_auc, color = "Validation"), linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(Train = "#ef8a62", Validation = "#67a9cf")) +
    labs(title = paste("AUC over training —", best_tag), y = "AUC", color = NULL) +
    # Prior elastic net AUC; see METHODS.md § "Relationship to the prior structural elastic net"
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.94, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
    annotate("text", x = max(log_df$epoch), y = 0.945,
             label = "Prior elastic net (AUC=0.94)", hjust = 1, size = 3, color = "grey40")

  p2 <- ggplot(log_df, aes(x = epoch)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = train_loss, color = "Train"), linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_line(aes(y = val_loss, color = "Validation"), linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(Train = "#ef8a62", Validation = "#67a9cf")) +
    labs(title = "Loss over training", y = "BCEWithLogits Loss", color = NULL)

  p3 <- ggplot(log_df, aes(x = epoch, y = auc_gap)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Train-Val AUC gap (overfitting monitor)", y = "AUC gap")

  save_fig(p1 / p2 / p3, "fig1c_training_curves", height = 10)
}
```

```{r fig1d-roc}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: predictions_{best_tag}.tsv — produced by evaluate.py
# Population: test set (chr 1,3,5,7, paralog-free)
roc_obj <- roc(preds$label, preds$prob, quiet = TRUE)
best_auc <- round(auc(roc_obj), 4)
best_auprc <- round(all_metrics$auprc[all_metrics$tag == best_tag], 4)

p_roc <- ggroc(roc_obj, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = sprintf("ROC Curve — %s (AUC = %s, AUPRC = %s)", best_tag, best_auc, best_auprc),
       subtitle = sprintf("Test set: %s transcripts (%s NMD, %s Control)",
                          format(n_test, big.mark = ","),
                          format(n_nmd, big.mark = ","),
                          format(n_ctrl, big.mark = ",")),
       x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3)

save_fig(p_roc, "fig1d_roc_curve")
```

**Key observations (data):**

```{r sec1-summary-stats, include=FALSE}
# Source: all_metrics (loaded in Section 0 from evaluate.py JSON files)
auc_stop2000 <- all_metrics$auc[all_metrics$tag == "atg500_stop2000"]
auprc_100_500 <- all_metrics$auprc[all_metrics$tag == "atg500_stop500"] -
                 all_metrics$auprc[all_metrics$tag == "atg500_stop100"]
```

- **STOP=500 is optimal by AUPRC** (AUC = `r best_auc`, AUPRC = `r best_auprc`). **(Data)** Performance declines at larger stop windows (STOP=2000 AUC = `r round(auc_stop2000, 3)`), suggesting the v5 architecture saturates its use of downstream context at ~500bp.
- **(Data)** **Stop window matters more than ATG window.** AUPRC peaks at STOP=500 across all ATG sizes. ATG=100 and ATG=500 perform similarly by AUPRC (< 0.002 difference at STOP=500), though ATG=500 has slightly higher AUC.
- **(Data)** The AUPRC gain from STOP=100→500 is +`r round(auprc_100_500, 3)`, confirming that extending the stop window from 100bp captures additional informative sequence context (particularly downstream splice junctions).

---

# 2. Feature Importance — What Drives Predictions? {#sec2-features}

**Question:** What features does the model rely on most to distinguish NMD from non-NMD?

**Population:** `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts. Branch-level importance from KernelSHAP (exact Shapley values, 500 background, all test samples). Per-position sequence SHAP from joint DeepSHAP (5 replicates, 500 background, all test samples).

```{r sec2-load-kernel-shap, include=FALSE}
# Load KernelSHAP branch decomposition
ks_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("kernel_shap_branch_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_kernel_shap <- file.exists(ks_path)
if (has_kernel_shap) {
  ks <- read.delim(ks_path)
  ks_nmd <- ks %>% filter(label > 0.5)
  ks_ctrl <- ks %>% filter(label < 0.5)
}

# Load SHAP profiles for sequence windows
atg_profile_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("shap_profile_atg_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
stop_profile_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("shap_profile_stop_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_shap_profiles <- file.exists(atg_profile_path) && file.exists(stop_profile_path)
if (has_shap_profiles) {
  atg_profile <- read.delim(atg_profile_path)
  stop_profile <- read.delim(stop_profile_path)
}
```

```{r prepare-unified-importance}
# ── Data provenance ──
# All importance from DeepSHAP: deepshap_summary_{tag}_run*.tsv — produced by deepshap.py.
#   Averaged across 5 replicates (different background/sample random seeds).
# Methods: METHODS.md § "DeepSHAP Sequence Interpretation"

# Feature labels for all branches
shap_feature_labels <- c(
  # Structural features
  "structural:n_downstream_ejc" = "Downstream EJC count",
  "structural:is_ref_cds" = "Is reference CDS",
  "structural:is_sqanti_cds" = "Is SQANTI/TD2 CDS",
  "structural:frac_start" = "Fractional start position",
  "structural:frac_stop" = "Fractional stop position",
  # Sequence channels (ATG branch)
  "atg:A" = "ATG: Adenine", "atg:C" = "ATG: Cytosine",
  "atg:G" = "ATG: Guanine", "atg:T" = "ATG: Thymine",
  "atg:junction" = "ATG: splice junction", "atg:rolling_gc" = "ATG: rolling GC",
  "atg:frame_0" = "ATG: frame pos 0", "atg:frame_1" = "ATG: frame pos 1",
  "atg:frame_2" = "ATG: frame pos 2",
  # Sequence channels (stop branch)
  "stop:A" = "Stop: Adenine", "stop:C" = "Stop: Cytosine",
  "stop:G" = "Stop: Guanine", "stop:T" = "Stop: Thymine",
  "stop:junction" = "Stop: splice junction", "stop:rolling_gc" = "Stop: rolling GC",
  "stop:frame_0" = "Stop: frame pos 0", "stop:frame_1" = "Stop: frame pos 1",
  "stop:frame_2" = "Stop: frame pos 2"
)

has_structural_shap <- has_shap && any(shap_summary$branch %in% c("structural", "joint_structural"))

# Add joint_* labels to feature mapping
shap_feature_labels <- c(shap_feature_labels,
  "joint_structural:n_downstream_ejc" = "Downstream EJC count",
  "joint_structural:is_ref_cds" = "Is reference CDS",
  "joint_structural:is_sqanti_cds" = "Is SQANTI/TD2 CDS",
  "joint_structural:frac_start" = "Fractional start position",
  "joint_structural:frac_stop" = "Fractional stop position",
  setNames(paste0("ATG: ", c("Adenine","Cytosine","Guanine","Thymine","splice junction","rolling GC","frame pos 0","frame pos 1","frame pos 2")),
           paste0("joint_atg:", c("A","C","G","T","junction","rolling_gc","frame_0","frame_1","frame_2"))),
  setNames(paste0("Stop: ", c("Adenine","Cytosine","Guanine","Thymine","splice junction","rolling GC","frame pos 0","frame pos 1","frame pos 2")),
           paste0("joint_stop:", c("A","C","G","T","junction","rolling_gc","frame_0","frame_1","frame_2")))
)

if (has_structural_shap) {
  # Unified importance from DeepSHAP for all feature types
  unified_imp <- shap_summary %>%
    mutate(raw_label = paste0(branch, ":", channel),
           feature = ifelse(raw_label %in% names(shap_feature_labels),
                            shap_feature_labels[raw_label], raw_label),
           mean_abs_gxi = mean_abs_shap_nmd,
           mean_gxi = NA_real_,
           feature_type = ifelse(grepl("structural", branch), "ORF structural", "Sequence"),
           level = ifelse(grepl("structural", branch), "orf_rank0", "sequence")) %>%
    select(feature, mean_abs_gxi, mean_gxi, feature_type, level) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_abs_gxi))

} else {
  # Fallback: minimal unified_imp from structural importance only
  unified_imp <- imp_rank %>%
    filter(stratification == "rank_0") %>%
    select(feature, mean_abs_gxi, mean_gxi) %>%
    mutate(feature_type = "ORF structural", level = "orf_rank0") %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_abs_gxi))
}
```

## 2.1 Branch-Level Importance {#sec2-branches}

```{r fig2a-branch-importance, eval=has_kernel_shap, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: kernel_shap_branch_{tag}.tsv — exact Shapley values for 3 branches
# Population: all test transcripts. KernelSHAP with 500 background, exact 2^3 coalitions.

ks_branch <- ks_nmd %>%
  summarise(ATG = mean(abs(shap_atg)),
            Stop = mean(abs(shap_stop)),
            Structural = mean(abs(shap_structural))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "branch", values_to = "mean_abs_shap") %>%
  mutate(total = sum(mean_abs_shap),
         pct = round(100 * mean_abs_shap / total, 1),
         branch = factor(branch, levels = c("Structural", "Stop", "ATG")))

p_branches <- ks_branch %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = branch, y = mean_abs_shap, fill = branch)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%\n(%.2f)", pct, mean_abs_shap)),
            vjust = -0.2, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Structural = "#d95f02", Stop = "#FF6B6B", ATG = "#4ECDC4")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title = sprintf("A. Branch-Level Attribution (NMD, n=%s, exact Shapley)",
                       format(nrow(ks_nmd), big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "KernelSHAP: 500 background, all 2^3 coalitions, residual = 0.",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean |SHAP|") +
  guides(fill = "none")

save_fig(p_branches, "fig2a_branch_importance", width = 8, height = 5)
```

<details><summary>Table 2a: Branch-level attribution</summary>
```{r tbl2a-branch, eval=has_kernel_shap}
render_dt(ks_branch %>% mutate(mean_abs_shap = round(mean_abs_shap, 4)),
          caption = "Table 2a: KernelSHAP branch decomposition (NMD)")
```
</details>

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The model's NMD prediction is driven primarily by **structural ORF features** (`r if(has_kernel_shap) sprintf("%.1f%%", ks_branch$pct[ks_branch$branch=="Structural"])` of total attribution), followed by the **stop codon branch** (`r if(has_kernel_shap) sprintf("%.1f%%", ks_branch$pct[ks_branch$branch=="Stop"])`) and the **ATG start codon branch** (`r if(has_kernel_shap) sprintf("%.1f%%", ks_branch$pct[ks_branch$branch=="ATG"])`). These are exact Shapley values that sum to the model prediction for every sample (residual = 0).

## 2.2 Structural Feature Importance {#sec2-structural}

Within the structural branch (61% of total attribution), the 5 ORF features are ranked by per-feature DeepSHAP (marginal, 500 background, 5 replicates, all test samples):

```{r fig2b-structural-importance, eval=has_kernel_shap, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
# Within-structural breakdown from marginal DeepSHAP (per-feature detail)
struct_imp <- unified_imp %>%
  filter(feature_type == "ORF structural") %>%
  mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = rev(feature)))

p_struct <- struct_imp %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_abs_gxi, y = feature)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#d95f02", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_abs_gxi)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title = "B. Structural Feature Importance (NMD, DeepSHAP)",
       subtitle = "Per-feature mean |SHAP| among NMD transcripts.",
       x = "Mean |SHAP|", y = NULL)

save_fig(p_struct, "fig2b_structural_importance", width = 8, height = 4)
```

<details><summary>Table 2b: Structural feature importance</summary>
```{r tbl2b-structural, eval=has_kernel_shap}
render_dt(struct_imp %>% mutate(mean_abs_gxi = round(mean_abs_gxi, 6)),
          caption = "Table 2b: Structural feature |SHAP| (NMD)")
```
</details>

**Variable definitions:**

- **`n_downstream_ejc`:** Number of exon-exon junctions downstream of this ORF's stop codon. >0 indicates a PTC by the EJC model.
- **`is_ref_cds`:** Whether this ORF matches the reference CDS reading frame.
- **`is_sqanti_cds`:** Whether this ORF matches the SQANTI/TD2 CDS prediction.
- **`frac_start` / `frac_stop`:** Fractional position of the ORF start/stop within the transcript.

## 2.3 Sequence SHAP Profiles {#sec2-sequence}

```{r fig2c-atg-profile, eval=has_shap_profiles, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: shap_profile_{atg,stop}_joint_{tag}.tsv — per-position |SHAP| from joint DeepSHAP
# Population: all test transcripts (NMD vs Control), joint DeepSHAP run 1

# ATG full window: total nucleotide |SHAP| by NMD/Control
atg_total <- atg_profile %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap),
               names_to = "class", values_to = "abs_shap") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(class == "nmd_abs_shap", "NMD", "Control")) %>%
  group_by(relative_position, class) %>%
  summarise(total_shap = sum(abs_shap), .groups = "drop")

p_atg_full <- atg_total %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = total_shap, color = class)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.1, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  annotate("text", x = -100, y = Inf, label = "5'UTR", size = 3.5, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = 100, y = Inf, label = "ORF body", size = 3.5, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  labs(title = sprintf("C. ATG Branch: Nucleotide |SHAP| Across Window (n=%s)",
                       format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Loess smooth. Sum of A+C+G+T |SHAP| per position. Dashed = ATG start codon.",
       x = "Position relative to ATG", y = "Total nucleotide |SHAP|", color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# ATG zoom ±15bp per nucleotide
p_atg_zoom <- atg_profile %>%
  filter(relative_position >= -15, relative_position <= 15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = nmd_abs_shap, color = channel)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -1.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = nuc_colors) +
  labs(title = "D. ATG ±15bp: Per-Nucleotide |SHAP| (NMD only)",
       subtitle = "Grey = ATG codon (A at -1, T at 0, G at +1). Note G periodicity (codon signal).",
       x = "Position relative to ATG", y = "Mean |SHAP|", color = "Nucleotide")

save_fig(p_atg_full / p_atg_zoom, "fig2c_atg_profile", width = 10, height = 8)
```

```{r fig2d-stop-profile, eval=has_shap_profiles, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# Stop full window
stop_total <- stop_profile %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap),
               names_to = "class", values_to = "abs_shap") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(class == "nmd_abs_shap", "NMD", "Control")) %>%
  group_by(relative_position, class) %>%
  summarise(total_shap = sum(abs_shap), .groups = "drop")

p_stop_full <- stop_total %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = total_shap, color = class)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.1, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  annotate("text", x = -150, y = Inf, label = "ORF body", size = 3.5, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = 150, y = Inf, label = "3'UTR", size = 3.5, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  labs(title = sprintf("E. Stop Branch: Nucleotide |SHAP| Across Window (n=%s)",
                       format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Loess smooth. Sum of A+C+G+T |SHAP| per position. Dashed = stop codon.",
       x = "Position relative to stop codon", y = "Total nucleotide |SHAP|", color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Stop zoom ±15bp per nucleotide
p_stop_zoom <- stop_profile %>%
  filter(relative_position >= -15, relative_position <= 15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = nmd_abs_shap, color = channel)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -1.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = nuc_colors) +
  labs(title = "F. Stop ±15bp: Per-Nucleotide |SHAP| (NMD only)",
       subtitle = "Grey = stop codon (T at -1, variable at 0 and +1). Clear stop codon spike.",
       x = "Position relative to stop codon", y = "Mean |SHAP|", color = "Nucleotide")

save_fig(p_stop_full / p_stop_zoom, "fig2d_stop_profile", width = 10, height = 8)
```

**Variable definitions (Panels C–F):**

- **Total nucleotide |SHAP| (Panels C, E):** Sum of mean |SHAP| across the 4 nucleotide channels (A, C, G, T) at each position. Higher values indicate positions where the model attends more strongly to nucleotide identity for NMD prediction.
- **Mean |SHAP| (Panels D, F):** Per-nucleotide-channel mean |SHAP| at each position for NMD samples. Letter height in the zoom panels shows which specific nucleotide carries the attribution signal.
- **Loess smooth (span=0.1):** Local polynomial regression to reveal position-dependent trends across the 500bp window. Ribbon = 95% CI.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The **stop branch** (Panel E) shows ~2x higher total nucleotide |SHAP| than the ATG branch (Panel C) at every position, consistent with the KernelSHAP branch decomposition (29% vs 10%).
- **(Data)** NMD transcripts have consistently higher sequence |SHAP| than Controls in both branches, reflecting the model's stronger "opinion" about NMD predictions.
- **(Data)** The **stop codon itself** (Panel F) shows a clear SHAP spike — the highest per-position attribution in either window. The T (red) at position -1 drops (invariant, no information), while the variable positions (0 and +1) show elevated G attribution, consistent with TGA preference.
- **(Data)** The **ATG branch** (Panel D) shows **strong period-3 periodicity** in the G channel (yellow) both upstream and downstream of the ATG. This reflects the model learning codon usage bias — G at the 3rd codon position (GC3) is a hallmark of vertebrate coding sequences. The lag-3 autocorrelation for G is 0.95 in the ORF body, with 90% of FFT power at period 3. The model detects reading frame composition from raw nucleotide sequence, in addition to the explicit frame-position encoding channels.
- **(Data)** The ATG branch SHAP is relatively **flat** across the window — no dramatic peak at the Kozak positions. The start codon context is a distributed signal, not concentrated at a single landmark.

---

# 3. Start Codon Signals {#sec3-start}

**Question:** What does the model learn from the sequence context around the ATG start codon?

**Metrics:** Section 3.1 uses DeepSHAP on the ATG branch (500bp window, all test samples, joint DeepSHAP averaged across 5 replicates). Subgroup-specific start codon analyses are in Section 6.

```{r sec3-stability-note, eval=has_stability, results='asis'}
nuc_cv <- shap_stability %>%
  filter(channel %in% c("A", "C", "G", "T")) %>% pull(cv_nmd)
junc_cv <- shap_stability %>%
  filter(channel == "junction", branch == "stop") %>% pull(cv_nmd)
cat(sprintf("**Attribution stability:** All DeepSHAP values in Sections 3-4 are means across 5 independent replicates. Nucleotide channel CVs = %.1f--%.1f%%; junction channel CV = %.1f%%.\n",
            100 * min(nuc_cv), 100 * max(nuc_cv), 100 * junc_cv))
```

## 3.1 Kozak Context {#sec3-kozak}

```{r fig3a-kozak-logo, eval=has_motif_logos, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: motif_logo_atg_{shap_run_tag}.tsv — produced by 07_motif_analysis.py
#   from deepshap_atg_{tag}.npz (DeepSHAP on rank-0 ORF ATG window)
# Methods: METHODS.md § "DeepSHAP Sequence Interpretation"

kozak_range <- motif_atg %>% filter(relative_position >= -11, relative_position <= 11)
positions <- sort(unique(kozak_range$relative_position))
nuc_order <- c("A", "C", "G", "T")

# Build matrices for ggseqlogo
nmd_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(positions),
                  dimnames = list(nuc_order, positions))
ctrl_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(positions),
                   dimnames = list(nuc_order, positions))
for (i in seq_along(positions)) {
  pos <- positions[i]
  for (j in seq_along(nuc_order)) {
    v_n <- kozak_range$nmd_mean_contrib[kozak_range$relative_position == pos &
                                         kozak_range$channel == nuc_order[j]]
    v_c <- kozak_range$ctrl_mean_contrib[kozak_range$relative_position == pos &
                                          kozak_range$channel == nuc_order[j]]
    if (length(v_n) == 1) nmd_mat[j, i] <- v_n
    if (length(v_c) == 1) ctrl_mat[j, i] <- v_c
  }
}

# Standard Kozak numbering: A of ATG = +1, no position 0
# Window center (relative_position = 0) is the T of ATG (middle nucleotide of codon).
# A of ATG is at relative_position = -1.
# Mapping: Kozak = relative_position + 2 for rel >= -1, else relative_position + 1
kozak_num <- ifelse(positions >= -1, positions + 2, positions + 1)
pos_labels <- ifelse(kozak_num > 0, paste0("+", kozak_num), as.character(kozak_num))
pos_labels[kozak_num == -3] <- "-3*"
pos_labels[kozak_num == -1] <- "-1*"
pos_labels[kozak_num == 4]  <- "+4*"
pos_labels[positions == -1] <- "A(+1)"
pos_labels[positions == 0]  <- "T(+2)"
pos_labels[positions == 1]  <- "G(+3)"

# Shared y-axis — generous padding (1.4x) to prevent ggseqlogo letter clipping
kz_ylim <- range(c(nmd_mat, ctrl_mat))
kz_ylim <- c(min(kz_ylim[1], 0), max(kz_ylim[2], 0))
kz_ylim <- kz_ylim * 1.4

p_kz_nmd <- ggseqlogo(nmd_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(positions), labels = pos_labels) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = kz_ylim) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = which(positions == -1) - 0.5, xmax = which(positions == 1) + 0.5,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = sprintf("ATG Context — NMD (n=%d, SHAP x input, +/-10bp)", motif_atg$n_nmd[1]),
       subtitle = "Letter height = SHAP attribution. Grey = ATG codon. * = canonical Kozak positions. Above zero = toward NMD.",
       x = "Position (A of ATG = +1)", y = "Mean SHAP x input") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))

p_kz_ctrl <- ggseqlogo(ctrl_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(positions), labels = pos_labels) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = kz_ylim) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = which(positions == -1) - 0.5, xmax = which(positions == 1) + 0.5,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = sprintf("ATG Context — Control (n=%d)", motif_atg$n_ctrl[1]),
       x = "Position (A of ATG = +1)", y = "Mean SHAP x input") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))

save_fig(p_kz_nmd / p_kz_ctrl, "fig3a_kozak_logo", width = 12, height = 8)
```

**Variable definitions:** Letter height = mean signed SHAP x input. Positive (upward) = pushes toward NMD. Numbering follows standard Kozak convention: A of ATG = +1. * marks canonical Kozak positions: **-3** (purine A/G preferred), **-1** (C preferred), **+4** (first nt after ATG, G preferred) **(literature: Kozak, 1987)**. Grey shading = ATG start codon (+1, +2, +3).

<details><summary>Table 3a: Kozak SHAP data</summary>
```{r table3a, eval=has_motif_logos}
render_dt(kozak_range %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 6))),
          caption = "Table 3a: Per-nucleotide SHAP at Kozak positions")
```
</details>

```{r sec3-kozak-stats, eval=has_motif_logos, include=FALSE}
# Extract specific Kozak SHAP values for inline text
# NOTE: Window center (relative_position=0) is T of ATG. A of ATG is at relative_position=-1.
# Kozak -3 = relative_position -4 (3 positions upstream of A)
# Kozak -1 = relative_position -2 (1 position upstream of A)
# Kozak +4 = relative_position +2 (1 position downstream of G)
kz_val <- function(pos, ch, class = "nmd") {
  col <- if (class == "nmd") "nmd_mean_contrib" else "ctrl_mean_contrib"
  v <- kozak_range[[col]][kozak_range$relative_position == pos & kozak_range$channel == ch]
  if (length(v) == 1) round(v, 3) else NA
}
# Kozak -3 (relative_position = -4)
kz_A_m3_nmd <- kz_val(-4, "A", "nmd")
kz_A_m3_ctrl <- kz_val(-4, "A", "ctrl")
# Kozak -1 (relative_position = -2)
kz_C_m1_nmd <- kz_val(-2, "C", "nmd")
kz_C_m1_ctrl <- kz_val(-2, "C", "ctrl")
# Kozak +4 (relative_position = +2)
kz_G_p4_nmd <- kz_val(2, "G", "nmd")
kz_G_p4_ctrl <- kz_val(2, "G", "ctrl")
kz_A_p4_nmd <- kz_val(2, "A", "nmd")
kz_T_p4_nmd <- kz_val(2, "T", "nmd")
# Overall +4 (sum of all nucleotide contributions)
kz_p4_nmd <- kz_val(2, "A", "nmd") + kz_val(2, "C", "nmd") + kz_val(2, "G", "nmd") + kz_val(2, "T", "nmd")
kz_p4_ctrl <- kz_val(2, "A", "ctrl") + kz_val(2, "C", "ctrl") + kz_val(2, "G", "ctrl") + kz_val(2, "T", "ctrl")
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** A at position -3 (the canonical Kozak purine position) is the strongest single-position signal in the ATG branch (`r kz_A_m3_nmd` in NMD, `r kz_A_m3_ctrl` in controls). C at position -1 is the second strongest (`r kz_C_m1_nmd` in NMD, `r kz_C_m1_ctrl` in controls).
- **(Data)** At position -3, the direction reverses between NMD (positive) and Control (slightly negative). At positions -1 and +4, the direction is the same in both classes (positive) but the magnitude is larger in NMD.
- **(Data)** Interestingly, the canonical G at position +4 has negative SHAP in NMD (`r kz_G_p4_nmd`) but positive in controls (`r kz_G_p4_ctrl`). The NMD-positive signal at +4 comes from A (`r kz_A_p4_nmd`) and T (`r kz_T_p4_nmd`), not the canonical G. This suggests the model is not simply recognizing strong Kozak context.
- **(Hypothesis)** The strong A at -3 signal in NMD may reflect a combination of genuine Kozak context recognition and the broader AT-rich composition bias of coding sequence upstream of the ATG (see Section 4.4 for an analogous GC composition effect at the stop codon). Disentangling these would require controlling for local nucleotide composition.

---

# 4. Stop Codon and 3'UTR Signals {#sec4-stop}

**Question:** What does the model learn from the sequence context around and downstream of the stop codon?

**Metric:** DeepSHAP on the stop branch (500-position window centered on the rank-0 ORF's stop codon, capturing ~250bp of 3'UTR). Same population as Section 3.

## 4.1 Stop Codon Identity {#sec4-stop-codon}

```{r fig4a-stop-logo, eval=has_motif_logos, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: motif_logos_stop_{shap_run_tag}.tsv — produced by 07_motif_analysis.py
#   from deepshap_stop_{tag}.npz (DeepSHAP on rank-0 ORF stop window)
# Population: ~2,000 DeepSHAP samples per replicate (run 1 shown)

# Stop codon logo: ±10bp around the stop codon
# Window center (relative_position = 0) = middle nucleotide of stop codon (2nd nt)
# Stop codon: T at -1, variable at 0, variable at +1
# Joint logo TSV has no landmark column; marginal has landmark == "stop_codon"
if ("landmark" %in% names(motif_stop)) {
  stop_logo <- motif_stop %>% filter(landmark == "stop_codon",
                                      relative_position >= -11, relative_position <= 11)
} else {
  stop_logo <- motif_stop %>% filter(relative_position >= -11, relative_position <= 11)
}
stop_positions <- sort(unique(stop_logo$relative_position))

# Build SHAP×input matrices
nmd_stop_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(stop_positions),
                       dimnames = list(nuc_order, stop_positions))
ctrl_stop_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(stop_positions),
                        dimnames = list(nuc_order, stop_positions))
for (i in seq_along(stop_positions)) {
  pos <- stop_positions[i]
  for (j in seq_along(nuc_order)) {
    v_n <- stop_logo$nmd_mean_contrib[stop_logo$relative_position == pos &
                                       stop_logo$channel == nuc_order[j]]
    v_c <- stop_logo$ctrl_mean_contrib[stop_logo$relative_position == pos &
                                        stop_logo$channel == nuc_order[j]]
    if (length(v_n) == 1) nmd_stop_mat[j, i] <- v_n
    if (length(v_c) == 1) ctrl_stop_mat[j, i] <- v_c
  }
}

# Position labels: same convention as Kozak — first codon nucleotide = +1, no zero.
# relative_position -1 = 1st nt of stop (T), 0 = 2nd nt, +1 = 3rd nt
# Mapping: stop_num = relative_position + 2 for rel >= -1, else rel + 1
stop_num <- ifelse(stop_positions >= -1, stop_positions + 2, stop_positions + 1)
stop_pos_labels <- ifelse(stop_num > 0, paste0("+", stop_num), as.character(stop_num))
stop_pos_labels[stop_positions == -1] <- "T(+1)"
stop_pos_labels[stop_positions == 0]  <- "+2"
stop_pos_labels[stop_positions == 1]  <- "+3"

# Shared y-axis limits — ggseqlogo letters can extend beyond the data value,
# so use generous padding (1.4x) to avoid clipping
sc_ylim <- range(c(nmd_stop_mat, ctrl_stop_mat))
sc_ylim <- c(min(sc_ylim[1], 0), max(sc_ylim[2], 0))
sc_ylim <- sc_ylim * 1.4

p_sc_nmd <- ggseqlogo(nmd_stop_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(stop_positions), labels = stop_pos_labels) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = sc_ylim) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = which(stop_positions == -1) - 0.5,
           xmax = which(stop_positions == 1) + 0.5,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Stop Codon Context — NMD (n=%d, SHAP x input, +/-10bp)",
                       stop_logo$n_nmd[1]),
       subtitle = "Letter height = SHAP attribution. Grey = stop codon (T + 2 variable positions). Above zero = toward NMD.",
       x = "Position (T of stop codon = +1)", y = "Mean SHAP x input") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))

p_sc_ctrl <- ggseqlogo(ctrl_stop_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(stop_positions), labels = stop_pos_labels) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = sc_ylim) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = which(stop_positions == -1) - 0.5,
           xmax = which(stop_positions == 1) + 0.5,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Stop Codon Context — Control (n=%d)", stop_logo$n_ctrl[1]),
       x = "Position (T of stop codon = +1)", y = "Mean SHAP x input") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))

save_fig(p_sc_nmd / p_sc_ctrl, "fig4a_stop_logo", width = 12, height = 8)
```

**Variable definitions:** Letter height = mean signed SHAP × input. Positive (upward) = pushes toward NMD. Grey shading = stop codon (3 positions: invariant T + 2 variable nucleotides encoding TGA/TAA/TAG). At the variable stop codon positions, letter heights reflect the model's preference: taller G at position 2 indicates TGA preference (positive SHAP), while A/G distribution at position 3 reflects TAA vs TAG distinction.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The stop codon variable positions (grey zone, positions 2-3) show clear nucleotide preference in NMD: G is positive at position 2 (favoring TGA), consistent with the stop codon frequency analysis below.
- **(Data)** Positions immediately upstream of the stop codon (CDS-proximal) show weak, mixed SHAP — the model does not strongly attend to the last few coding nucleotides.
- **(Data)** Positions downstream of the stop codon (+2 onward, 3'UTR) show the beginning of the junction/nucleotide signals analyzed in detail in Sections 4.2-4.6.

```{r fig4a-stop-codon-freq, eval=has_subgroup_shap, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: subgroup_stop_codon_shap_{tag}.tsv — produced by 08_export_subgroup_deepshap_tsv.py
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Subgroup-Specific DeepSHAP Analysis"
# Population: ~2,000 DeepSHAP samples per replicate, pooled across 5 runs.
# Stop codon identity decoded from one-hot encoding at the window center.

# Aggregate subgroups into NMD vs Control
# Weight NMD subgroups equally (each run samples proportionally; subgroup sizes
# within DeepSHAP runs are already proportional to test prevalence)
sc_pop <- sg_stop_cd %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(subgroup == "Control", "Control", "NMD")) %>%
  group_by(class, stop_codon) %>%
  summarise(pct = mean(pct_mean),
            mean_shap = mean(mean_signed_shap_mean),
            .groups = "drop")

# Reconstruct approximate counts for chi-squared test
# (based on DeepSHAP subsample of ~2,000 transcripts per run)
n_shap_nmd <- shap_n_nmd
n_shap_ctrl <- 2000 - shap_n_nmd
sc_pop$approx_n <- ifelse(sc_pop$class == "NMD",
                           round(sc_pop$pct * n_shap_nmd),
                           round(sc_pop$pct * n_shap_ctrl))

# Approximate chi-squared from reconstructed counts
cont_table <- sc_pop %>%
  select(class, stop_codon, approx_n) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = approx_n) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("stop_codon") %>%
  as.matrix()
chi_test <- chisq.test(cont_table)

sc_pop$stop_codon <- factor(sc_pop$stop_codon, levels = c("TGA", "TAA", "TAG"))

# Pairwise Fisher exact tests for each stop codon (NMD vs Control)
pairwise_p <- sapply(c("TGA", "TAA", "TAG"), function(sc) {
  n_nmd_sc <- cont_table[sc, "NMD"]
  n_ctrl_sc <- cont_table[sc, "Control"]
  n_nmd_other <- sum(cont_table[, "NMD"]) - n_nmd_sc
  n_ctrl_other <- sum(cont_table[, "Control"]) - n_ctrl_sc
  fisher.test(matrix(c(n_nmd_sc, n_ctrl_sc, n_nmd_other, n_ctrl_other), nrow = 2))$p.value
})

# Annotation data frame for pairwise p-values
sc_annot <- data.frame(
  stop_codon = factor(c("TGA", "TAA", "TAG"), levels = c("TGA", "TAA", "TAG")),
  y = c(max(sc_pop$pct[sc_pop$stop_codon == "TGA"]),
        max(sc_pop$pct[sc_pop$stop_codon == "TAA"]),
        max(sc_pop$pct[sc_pop$stop_codon == "TAG"])) + 0.06,
  label = sprintf("p=%s", ifelse(pairwise_p < 0.001, formatC(pairwise_p, format = "e", digits = 1),
                                  formatC(pairwise_p, format = "f", digits = 3)))
)

p_sc <- sc_pop %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = stop_codon, y = pct, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", 100*pct)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
  geom_text(data = sc_annot, aes(x = stop_codon, y = y, label = label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3, color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 0.78)) +
  labs(title = "Stop Codon Usage: NMD vs Control (rank-0 ORF)",
       subtitle = sprintf("Chi-sq = %.1f, p = %.1e (overall); pairwise Fisher exact tests shown per codon.",
                          as.numeric(chi_test$statistic), chi_test$p.value),
       x = "Stop codon", y = "Frequency", fill = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_sc, "fig4a_stop_codon", width = 7, height = 6)
```

**Variable definitions:** TGA, TAA, TAG are the three standard stop codons. TGA has the highest readthrough rate; TAA is the most efficient **(literature: Cridge et al., 2018)**. Bars show frequency among rank-0 ORFs in the DeepSHAP sample set. Chi-squared test is approximate (computed from reconstructed counts based on subgroup-weighted frequencies).

**Observations:**

```{r sec4-stop-codon-shap, eval=has_subgroup_shap, include=FALSE}
tga_nmd <- sc_pop %>% filter(class == "NMD", stop_codon == "TGA")
taa_nmd <- sc_pop %>% filter(class == "NMD", stop_codon == "TAA")
tag_nmd <- sc_pop %>% filter(class == "NMD", stop_codon == "TAG")
tga_ctrl <- sc_pop %>% filter(class == "Control", stop_codon == "TGA")
```

```{r sec4-stop-obs, eval=has_subgroup_shap, results='asis'}
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** TGA is enriched in NMD (%.1f%% vs %.1f%% in Control). TAA and TAG are both depleted in NMD. Mean signed SHAP (NMD): TGA = %.4f, TAA = %.4f, TAG = %.4f — confirming TGA pushes toward NMD while TAA and TAG push away.\n",
    100*tga_nmd$pct, 100*tga_ctrl$pct, tga_nmd$mean_shap, taa_nmd$mean_shap, tag_nmd$mean_shap))
```

### 4.1.1 Stop Codon SHAP: NMD vs Control {#sec4-stop-codon-nmd-ctrl}

```{r fig4a-stop-codon-nmd-ctrl, eval=has_subgroup_shap, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: subgroup_stop_codon_shap_{tag}.tsv — produced by 08_export_subgroup_deepshap_tsv.py
# Aggregated to NMD vs Control for population-level view.

sg_sc_class <- sg_stop_cd %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(subgroup == "Control", "Control", "NMD")) %>%
  group_by(class, stop_codon) %>%
  summarise(signed_shap = mean(mean_signed_shap_mean),
            pct = mean(pct_mean),
            .groups = "drop")

p_sc_class <- ggplot(sg_sc_class, aes(x = stop_codon, y = signed_shap, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = "Stop Codon Signed SHAP: NMD vs Control",
       subtitle = "Mean signed SHAP at stop codon variable positions (5-run pooled)",
       x = "Stop codon", y = "Signed SHAP (variable positions)", fill = NULL)
save_fig(p_sc_class, "fig4a_stop_codon_nmd_ctrl", width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r tbl4a-stop-codon-nmd-ctrl, eval=has_subgroup_shap}
render_dt(
  sg_sc_class %>%
    mutate(signed_shap = round(signed_shap, 5), pct = round(pct, 3)),
  caption = "Table 4a: Stop codon identity — NMD vs Control (5-run pooled)")
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** TGA has positive signed SHAP in both NMD and Control, but the NMD magnitude is approximately 2x larger. TAA and TAG both have negative SHAP. This confirms the model has learned that TGA (the stop codon with the highest readthrough rate) is associated with NMD.
- **(Data)** The TGA-positive / TAA,TAG-negative pattern is sign-consistent across all 5 DeepSHAP replicates.

## 4.2 Splice Site Motif at 3'UTR Junctions {#sec4-junction-motif}

```{r fig4b-junction-logo, eval=has_motif_logos, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: motif_logos_stop_{shap_run_tag}.tsv — produced by 07_motif_analysis.py
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Landmark motif analysis"
# Population: NMD samples with >=1 exon junction in visible 3'UTR (n varies by transcript)

junc_sub <- motif_stop %>% filter(landmark == "first_3utr_junction")

if (nrow(junc_sub) > 0) {
  junc_positions <- sort(unique(junc_sub$relative_position))

  junc_nmd_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(junc_positions),
                          dimnames = list(nuc_order, junc_positions))
  junc_ctrl_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(junc_positions),
                           dimnames = list(nuc_order, junc_positions))
  for (i in seq_along(junc_positions)) {
    pos <- junc_positions[i]
    for (j in seq_along(nuc_order)) {
      v_n <- junc_sub$nmd_mean_contrib[junc_sub$relative_position == pos &
                                        junc_sub$channel == nuc_order[j]]
      v_c <- junc_sub$ctrl_mean_contrib[junc_sub$relative_position == pos &
                                         junc_sub$channel == nuc_order[j]]
      if (length(v_n) == 1) junc_nmd_mat[j, i] <- v_n
      if (length(v_c) == 1) junc_ctrl_mat[j, i] <- v_c
    }
  }

  # Standard splice site numbering: no position 0.
  # -1 = last nt of upstream exon, +1 = first nt of downstream exon.
  # Our relative_position 0 = standard +1, relative_position -1 = standard -1.
  junc_pos_labels <- ifelse(junc_positions >= 0,
                             as.character(junc_positions + 1),
                             as.character(junc_positions))
  junc_pos_labels[junc_positions == -2] <- "-2\n(C)"
  junc_pos_labels[junc_positions == -1] <- "-1\n(A)"
  junc_pos_labels[junc_positions == 0]  <- "+1\njxn(G)"
  junc_pos_labels[junc_positions == 1]  <- "+2\n(G)"
  junc_pos_labels[junc_positions == 2]  <- "+3\n(T)"

  # Only show labels at every 3rd position + the key ones to avoid crowding
  show_idx <- c(which(junc_positions %% 3 == 0),
                which(junc_positions %in% c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)))
  show_idx <- sort(unique(show_idx))
  sparse_labels <- rep("", length(junc_positions))
  sparse_labels[show_idx] <- junc_pos_labels[show_idx]

  n_nmd_junc <- junc_sub$n_nmd[1]
  jxn_x <- which(junc_positions == 0)

  # Compute shared y-axis limits across both panels
  junc_ylim <- range(c(junc_nmd_mat, junc_ctrl_mat))
  junc_ylim <- c(min(junc_ylim[1], 0), max(junc_ylim[2], 0))

  p_junc_nmd <- ggseqlogo(junc_nmd_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(junc_positions), labels = sparse_labels) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = junc_ylim) +
    annotate("segment", x = jxn_x - 0.5, xend = jxn_x - 0.5,
             y = -Inf, yend = Inf, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    annotate("text", x = jxn_x - 2, y = junc_ylim[2] * 0.9,
             label = "upstream exon", size = 2.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 1) +
    annotate("text", x = jxn_x + 2, y = junc_ylim[2] * 0.9,
             label = "downstream exon", size = 2.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0) +
    labs(title = sprintf("First 3'UTR Exon Junction — NMD (n=%d, +/-15bp)", n_nmd_junc),
         subtitle = "Red line = exon-exon junction. Pattern: ...A | G(G)T... All positive = pushes toward NMD.",
         x = "Position relative to junction (bp)", y = "Mean SHAP x input")

  p_junc_ctrl <- ggseqlogo(junc_ctrl_mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(junc_positions), labels = sparse_labels) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = junc_ylim) +
    annotate("segment", x = jxn_x - 0.5, xend = jxn_x - 0.5,
             y = -Inf, yend = Inf, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = sprintf("First 3'UTR Exon Junction — Control (n=%d)", junc_sub$n_ctrl[1]),
         x = "Position relative to junction (bp)", y = "Mean SHAP x input")

  save_fig(p_junc_nmd / p_junc_ctrl, "fig4b_junction_logo", height = 8)
}
```

**Variable definitions:** Each sample contributes its own first 3'UTR junction location, aligned to position 0. In the processed mRNA (which is what the model sees), the junction is the ligation point between exons after intron removal. The dominant pattern is **...A | G(G)...**: position -1 = A (65% freq, last base of upstream exon), position 0 = G (82%, first base of downstream exon). After intron removal, the mRNA junction reflects the exonic bases flanking the splice site. The A at position -1 and G at position +1 are the terminal/initial exon bases, consistent with the preference for these nucleotides at mammalian exon boundaries. n varies because not all transcripts have junctions within the visible stop window.

## 4.3 EJC Dose-Response and Ordinal Importance {#sec4-ejc}

```{r fig4c-ejc-analysis, eval=has_junc_ord, fig.width=10, fig.height=9}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Panel A: predictions_{tag}.tsv + selected_orfs.tsv — structural features
#   Source script: 04_interpret_attention.py; Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis"
#   Population: all NMD test transcripts
# Panel B: junction_by_ordinal_{shap_run_tag}.tsv — DeepSHAP per-junction attribution
#   Source script: 06_export_deepshap_tsv.py; Methods: METHODS.md § "DeepSHAP Sequence Interpretation"
#   Population: ~NMD samples from DeepSHAP subset (proportional to test prevalence)

ejc_levels <- c("EJC 1", "EJC 2", "EJC 3", "EJC 4", "EJC 5+")

# Panel A: P(NMD) by EJC count
rank0_ejc <- test_rank0 %>%
  filter(label == 1) %>%
  mutate(n_ejc_capped = pmin(n_downstream_ejc, 5),
         ejc_label = ifelse(n_ejc_capped == 5, "5+", as.character(n_ejc_capped)))

ejc_summary <- rank0_ejc %>%
  group_by(n_ejc_capped, ejc_label) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), mean_prob = mean(prob), se = sd(prob)/sqrt(n()), .groups = "drop")

p_dose <- ejc_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ejc_label, y = mean_prob)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#ef8a62", width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_prob - se, ymax = pmin(mean_prob + se, 1)), width = 0.15) +
  geom_text(aes(y = 0.03, label = sprintf("n=%d", n)), size = 3.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.02), labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title = "A. NMD Prediction by Downstream EJC Count",
       subtitle = sprintf("Mean P(NMD) +/- SE. n = %s NMD transcripts, STOP=500 model.",
                          format(n_nmd, big.mark = ",")),
       x = "Number of downstream EJCs (rank-0 ORF)", y = "Mean P(NMD)") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))

# Panel B: SHAP by ordinal (loess smoothed)
nmd_raw <- junc_raw %>%
  filter(class == "NMD") %>%
  mutate(ejc_label = factor(ejc_label, levels = ejc_levels))

sufficient <- nmd_raw %>% count(ejc_label) %>% filter(n >= 20) %>% pull(ejc_label)

p_shap_ord <- nmd_raw %>%
  filter(ejc_label %in% sufficient) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = distance_from_stop, y = signed_shap, color = ejc_label)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.5, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ejc_pal) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, 100)) +
  labs(title = "B. Junction SHAP by EJC Order and Distance (NMD, DeepSHAP)",
       subtitle = sprintf("Loess-smoothed signed SHAP with 95%% CI. n = %d NMD samples in DeepSHAP subset.",
                          sum(junc_raw$class == "NMD" & !duplicated(junc_raw$sample_idx))),
       x = "Distance from stop codon (bp)", y = "Signed SHAP at junction", color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "right", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))

save_fig(p_dose / p_shap_ord, "fig4c_ejc_analysis", width = 10, height = 9)
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **Panel A — n_downstream_ejc:** Number of exon-exon junctions downstream of the rank-0 ORF's stop codon (from structural features, all `r format(n_nmd, big.mark = ",")` NMD test transcripts).
- **Panel B — EJC 1/2/3/4:** The 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th exon-exon junction encountered when moving downstream from the stop codon. Each junction's position varies across transcripts. Loess smoothing shows the trend in |SHAP| as a function of distance.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The largest prediction jump is 0→1 EJC (P(NMD) from `r sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*ejc_summary$mean_prob[ejc_summary$n_ejc_capped==0])` to `r sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*ejc_summary$mean_prob[ejc_summary$n_ejc_capped==1])`). Additional EJCs provide diminishing but real gains: 2 EJCs = `r sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*ejc_summary$mean_prob[ejc_summary$n_ejc_capped==2])`, 3 = `r sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*ejc_summary$mean_prob[ejc_summary$n_ejc_capped==3])`, 5+ = `r sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*ejc_summary$mean_prob[ejc_summary$n_ejc_capped==5])`.
- **(Data)** Per-junction SHAP magnitude is roughly constant across distance and ordinal — the 1st and 4th EJC contribute similarly when present. The model treats each EJC as an additive NMD signal.

## 4.4 GC Enrichment Signal at Coding-UTR Boundaries {#sec4-gc}

```{r fig4d-gc-shap-enrichment, eval=has_shap_tsv && has_gc, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# SHAP sources:
#   ATG: deepshap_atg_positional_{tag}.tsv — per-position DeepSHAP (shap_atg_pos, loaded above)
#   Stop: deepshap_stop_regional_{tag}.tsv — 25bp-binned DeepSHAP (shap_stop_reg, loaded above)
#   Produced by: 06_export_deepshap_tsv.py from deepshap.py outputs
# Raw GC source:
#   Stop: gc_content_across_stop_window_{tag}.tsv (gc_data, loaded above)
#   ATG: computed here from nmd_freq/ctrl_freq columns in shap_atg_pos
# Methods: METHODS.md § "DeepSHAP Sequence Interpretation"
# Population: ~2,000 DeepSHAP samples (~shap_n_nmd NMD, ~(2000-shap_n_nmd) Control) per replicate

# ── Panel A: ATG window — GC SHAP fraction ──
# GC SHAP fraction = (|SHAP_G| + |SHAP_C|) / (|SHAP_A| + |SHAP_C| + |SHAP_G| + |SHAP_T|)
atg_shap_wide <- shap_atg_pos %>%
  filter(channel %in% c("A", "C", "G", "T")) %>%
  select(relative_position, channel, nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = channel,
              values_from = c(nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap)) %>%
  mutate(
    nmd_gc_frac  = (nmd_abs_shap_G + nmd_abs_shap_C) /
                   (nmd_abs_shap_A + nmd_abs_shap_C + nmd_abs_shap_G + nmd_abs_shap_T),
    ctrl_gc_frac = (ctrl_abs_shap_G + ctrl_abs_shap_C) /
                   (ctrl_abs_shap_A + ctrl_abs_shap_C + ctrl_abs_shap_G + ctrl_abs_shap_T)
  )

atg_gc_shap <- atg_shap_wide %>%
  select(relative_position, nmd_gc_frac, ctrl_gc_frac) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nmd_gc_frac, ctrl_gc_frac),
               names_to = "class", values_to = "gc_shap_frac") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(class == "nmd_gc_frac", "NMD", "Control"))

p_a <- atg_gc_shap %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = gc_shap_frac, color = class)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  annotate("text", x = -25, y = max(atg_gc_shap$gc_shap_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "5'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 25, y = max(atg_gc_shap$gc_shap_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "ORF body\n(coding)", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = sprintf("A. GC SHAP Fraction — ATG Window (ATG=%d, n=%s samples)",
                       best_atg, format(2000, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Loess smooth. Dashed line = start codon. Dotted line = 0.5 (equal GC/AT attribution).",
       x = "Position relative to ATG (bp)", y = "GC SHAP fraction",
       color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_a, "fig4d_gc_shap_atg", width = 10, height = 5)

# ── Panel B: ATG window — raw GC content ──
atg_gc_raw <- shap_atg_pos %>%
  filter(channel %in% c("G", "C")) %>%
  group_by(relative_position) %>%
  summarise(nmd_gc = sum(nmd_freq), ctrl_gc = sum(ctrl_freq), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nmd_gc, ctrl_gc),
               names_to = "class", values_to = "gc_content") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(class == "nmd_gc", "NMD", "Control"))

p_b <- atg_gc_raw %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = gc_content, color = class)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  annotate("text", x = -25, y = max(atg_gc_raw$gc_content, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "5'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 25, y = max(atg_gc_raw$gc_content, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "ORF body\n(coding)", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = sprintf("B. Raw GC Content — ATG Window (ATG=%d, n=%s samples)",
                       best_atg, format(2000, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Loess smooth. Dashed line = start codon. GC = freq(G) + freq(C) at each position.",
       x = "Position relative to ATG (bp)", y = "GC content",
       color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_b, "fig4d_gc_raw_atg", width = 10, height = 5)

# ── Panel C: Stop window — GC SHAP fraction ──
stop_shap_wide <- shap_stop_reg %>%
  filter(channel %in% c("A", "C", "G", "T")) %>%
  mutate(rel_mid = (rel_start + rel_end) / 2) %>%
  select(rel_mid, channel, nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = channel,
              values_from = c(nmd_abs_shap, ctrl_abs_shap)) %>%
  mutate(
    nmd_gc_frac  = (nmd_abs_shap_G + nmd_abs_shap_C) /
                   (nmd_abs_shap_A + nmd_abs_shap_C + nmd_abs_shap_G + nmd_abs_shap_T),
    ctrl_gc_frac = (ctrl_abs_shap_G + ctrl_abs_shap_C) /
                   (ctrl_abs_shap_A + ctrl_abs_shap_C + ctrl_abs_shap_G + ctrl_abs_shap_T)
  )

stop_gc_shap <- stop_shap_wide %>%
  select(rel_mid, nmd_gc_frac, ctrl_gc_frac) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nmd_gc_frac, ctrl_gc_frac),
               names_to = "class", values_to = "gc_shap_frac") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(class == "nmd_gc_frac", "NMD", "Control"))

p_c <- stop_gc_shap %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rel_mid, y = gc_shap_frac, color = class)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  annotate("text", x = -200, y = max(stop_gc_shap$gc_shap_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "ORF body\n(coding)", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 250, y = max(stop_gc_shap$gc_shap_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "3'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = sprintf("C. GC SHAP Fraction — Stop Window (STOP=%d, n=%s samples)",
                       best_stop, format(2000, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Loess smooth over 25bp bins. Dashed line = stop codon. Dotted line = 0.5 (equal GC/AT attribution).",
       x = "Position relative to stop codon (bp)", y = "GC SHAP fraction",
       color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_c, "fig4d_gc_shap_stop", width = 10, height = 5)

# ── Panel D: Stop window — raw GC content (existing gc_data) ──
p_d <- gc_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rel_mid, y = mean_gc, color = class)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_gc - se_gc, ymax = mean_gc + se_gc, fill = class),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  annotate("text", x = -200, y = 0.52, label = "ORF body\n(coding)", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 250, y = 0.52, label = "3'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = sprintf("D. Raw GC Content — Stop Window (STOP=%d, n=%s samples)",
                       best_stop, format(2000, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "50bp sliding window. Dashed line = stop codon. Ribbon = SE across samples.",
       x = "Position relative to stop codon (bp)", y = "GC content",
       color = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

save_fig(p_d, "fig4d_gc_raw_stop", width = 10, height = 5)
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **GC SHAP fraction:** (|SHAP_G| + |SHAP_C|) / (|SHAP_A| + |SHAP_C| + |SHAP_G| + |SHAP_T|). The proportion of total nucleotide-channel DeepSHAP attribution at each position attributable to G and C channels. Values > 0.5 indicate GC channels carry disproportionate model attribution; values < 0.5 indicate AT channels dominate.
- **GC content:** Fraction of G + C nucleotides at each position, computed from nucleotide frequencies in the DeepSHAP sample set (Panels B, D) or from 50bp sliding windows (Panel D).
- **Smoothing:** ATG panels (A, B) use loess (span = 0.3) to reduce per-position noise. Stop SHAP panel (C) uses pre-aggregated 25bp bins. Stop GC content panel (D) uses pre-aggregated 50bp sliding windows with 10bp step.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** At the ATG boundary (Panels A–B), both GC SHAP fraction and raw GC content rise after the start codon, reflecting the transition from AT-rich 5'UTR into GC-rich coding sequence. The model's attribution pattern mirrors the underlying composition shift.
- **(Data)** At the stop boundary (Panels C–D), both GC SHAP fraction and raw GC content drop after the stop codon, reflecting the coding → 3'UTR transition. In controls, the drop is sharp (~48% → ~36% in raw GC). In NMD transcripts, the drop is attenuated (~50% → ~45%).
- **(Hypothesis)** The attenuated GC drop in NMD transcripts at the stop codon is consistent with "UTR-ization" — when a PTC truncates the reading frame, the sequence between PTC and normal stop is structurally coding (GC-rich) but functionally 3'UTR. Both the raw composition (Panel D) and the model attribution (Panel C) capture this anomaly, confirming the model learned the coding/UTR compositional boundary as a discriminative signal.
- **(Data)** The correspondence between SHAP-based panels (A, C) and raw-sequence panels (B, D) confirms the model is responding to nucleotide composition rather than specific motifs at these scales.

## 4.5 Poly(A) Signal {#sec4-polya}

