```{r fig4e-polya, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: polya_sqanti_test_{tag}.tsv — poly(A) motif annotations from SQANTI3
#   SQANTI identifies poly(A) motifs near the transcript 3' end using established
#   motif sequences (AATAAA and 11 known variants). polyA_dist = distance of motif
#   from transcript end (median ~ -20 nt).
# See METHODS.md § "Poly(A) Signal" (if documented) or SQANTI3 documentation
# Population: all test transcripts

pa_sqanti_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("polya_sqanti_test_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_pa_sqanti <- file.exists(pa_sqanti_path)

if (has_pa_sqanti) {
  pa_sq <- read.delim(pa_sqanti_path) %>%
    mutate(class = ifelse(label == 1, "NMD", "Control"),
           has_polya = polyA_motif_found == "True")

  # Panel A: poly(A) motif prevalence by class
  pa_prev <- pa_sq %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), n_with = sum(has_polya, na.rm = TRUE),
              pct = mean(has_polya, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  # Fisher test (exclude NAs)
  pa_sq_clean <- pa_sq %>% filter(!is.na(has_polya))
  ft_pa <- fisher.test(table(pa_sq_clean$has_polya, pa_sq_clean$class))

  p_pa1 <- pa_prev %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = class, y = pct, fill = class)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d/%d\n(%.1f%%)", n_with, n, 100*pct)),
              vjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(title = "A. Poly(A) Motif Prevalence (SQANTI3 annotation)",
         subtitle = sprintf("Fisher exact: OR=%.2f, %s. Motif identified near transcript 3' end.",
                            ft_pa$estimate,
                            ifelse(ft_pa$p.value < 0.001, sprintf("p=%.1e", ft_pa$p.value),
                                   sprintf("p=%.3f", ft_pa$p.value))),
         x = NULL, y = "Fraction with poly(A) motif", fill = NULL) +
    guides(fill = "none")

  # Panel B: motif type distribution among those with poly(A)
  top_motifs <- c("AATAAA", "ATTAAA", "AGTAAA", "TATAAA")
  pa_motif_freq <- pa_sq %>%
    filter(has_polya, polyA_motif %in% top_motifs) %>%
    mutate(polyA_motif = factor(polyA_motif, levels = top_motifs)) %>%
    group_by(class, polyA_motif) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>% ungroup()

  # Chi-squared test on motif type distribution (NMD vs Control)
  motif_cont <- pa_motif_freq %>%
    select(class, polyA_motif, n) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
    tibble::column_to_rownames("polyA_motif") %>%
    as.matrix()
  chi_motif <- chisq.test(motif_cont)

  p_pa2 <- pa_motif_freq %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = polyA_motif, y = pct, fill = class)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%", 100*pct)),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
    labs(title = "B. Poly(A) Motif Type Distribution",
         subtitle = sprintf("Top 4 motifs shown. Chi-sq = %.1f, p = %s.",
                            as.numeric(chi_motif$statistic),
                            ifelse(chi_motif$p.value < 0.001, sprintf("%.1e", chi_motif$p.value),
                                   sprintf("%.3f", chi_motif$p.value))),
         x = "Poly(A) motif", y = "Fraction (within poly(A)+ transcripts)", fill = NULL) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  save_fig(p_pa1 / p_pa2, "fig4e_polya", width = 9, height = 8)
}
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **Poly(A) motif detection:** SQANTI3 identifies poly(A) signal motifs near the transcript 3' end (median distance = ~20 nt upstream of the 3' terminus). This is the established transcript-level annotation, not a window-based search.
- **AATAAA:** Canonical poly(A) signal. ATTAAA, AGTAAA, TATAAA are the most common variants.
- **Panel A** shows overall prevalence; **Panel B** shows the motif type breakdown among transcripts with a detected motif.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** Poly(A) motif prevalence is nearly identical between NMD (`r if(has_pa_sqanti) round(100 * pa_prev$pct[pa_prev$class == "NMD"], 1) else "N/A"`%) and Control (`r if(has_pa_sqanti) round(100 * pa_prev$pct[pa_prev$class == "Control"], 1) else "N/A"`%). The canonical AATAAA accounts for `r if(has_pa_sqanti) round(100 * pa_motif_freq$pct[pa_motif_freq$class == "NMD" & pa_motif_freq$polyA_motif == "AATAAA"], 0) else "N/A"`% of motifs in NMD.
- **(Data)** Since poly(A) signals are typically located near the 3' end of the transcript (median ~20 nt from the terminus), they fall outside the model's stop window for most transcripts. The similar prevalence in NMD and Control confirms that poly(A) signal presence at the transcript level does not distinguish the two classes. Any model signal related to poly(A) motifs would need to come from the rare cases where the 3' end falls within the stop window.

<details><summary>DeepSHAP stability table (5 replicates)</summary>
```{r shap-stability-table, eval=has_stability}
render_dt(shap_stability %>%
            select(branch, channel, mean_abs_shap_nmd_mean, mean_abs_shap_nmd_std, cv_nmd) %>%
            mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 4))) %>%
            rename(mean_shap_nmd = mean_abs_shap_nmd_mean,
                   sd_shap_nmd = mean_abs_shap_nmd_std),
          caption = "DeepSHAP stability: mean |SHAP| and CV across 5 replicates (NMD class)")
```
</details>

## 4.6 Downstream Junction SHAP: NMD vs Control {#sec4-junction-shap}

```{r fig4f-junction-nmd-ctrl, eval=has_subgroup_shap, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: subgroup_junction_downstream_shap_{tag}.tsv — produced by 08_export_subgroup_deepshap_tsv.py
# Aggregated to NMD vs Control.

sg_junc_class <- sg_junc %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(subgroup == "Control", "Control", "NMD")) %>%
  group_by(class) %>%
  summarise(junction_shap = mean(mean_junction_shap_mean),
            ci_lo = mean(mean_junction_shap_ci_lo),
            ci_hi = mean(mean_junction_shap_ci_hi),
            frac_with_junction = mean(frac_with_junction_mean),
            mean_junc_count = mean(mean_junc_count_mean),
            .groups = "drop")

p_junc_class <- ggplot(sg_junc_class, aes(x = class, y = junction_shap, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = "Junction Channel SHAP Downstream of Stop: NMD vs Control",
       subtitle = "Mean signed SHAP in 3'UTR region (5-run pooled, 95% CI)",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean junction SHAP", fill = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none")
save_fig(p_junc_class, "fig4f_junction_nmd_ctrl", width = 8, height = 5)
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** NMD transcripts have substantially stronger downstream junction SHAP than Controls — the junction channel is the primary sequence-level EJC signature in the stop branch.
- **(Data)** The NMD signal reflects exon-exon junctions downstream of the stop codon, which are the physical basis for NMD (EJC-mediated recognition of premature termination).

---

# 5. ORF Selection — What Does the Attention Mechanism Reveal? {#sec5-attention}

**Question:** Does the model select a single dominant ORF or distribute attention across multiple?

**Population:** `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts, each with up to 5 candidate ORFs.

```{r fig5a-entropy, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: attention_entropy_{tag}.tsv — produced by 04_interpret_attention.py
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis > Attention entropy"

entropy_summary <- attn_entropy %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(label == 1, "NMD", "Control"),
         effective_orfs = 2^attn_entropy)

p_entropy <- entropy_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = attn_entropy, fill = class)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = "Attention Entropy: NMD vs Control",
       subtitle = sprintf("Shannon entropy (bits). Higher = more distributed. n = %s transcripts.",
                          format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       x = "Attention entropy (bits)", y = "Density", fill = NULL)

save_fig(p_entropy, "fig4a_entropy")
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **Attention entropy:** Shannon entropy (base 2) of the attention weight vector. 0 bits = all attention on one ORF. log2(K) bits = uniform. "Effective ORFs" = 2^entropy.
- **Attention weight:** Softmax-normalized score per ORF (sums to 1). Measures ORF selection, NOT prediction direction.
- **ORF rank:** Priority order: 0 = ref CDS ORF (if available), then SQANTI CDS, then filled by descending Kozak score.

<details><summary>Table 5a: Entropy statistics</summary>

```{r table4-entropy}
ent_stats <- entropy_summary %>%
  group_by(class) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), mean_entropy = round(mean(attn_entropy), 3),
            median_entropy = round(median(attn_entropy), 3),
            mean_eff_orfs = round(mean(effective_orfs), 2),
            pct_rank0_dominant = round(100 * mean(argmax_orf == 0), 1),
            .groups = "drop")
render_dt(ent_stats, caption = "Table 5a: Attention entropy by NMD status")
```
</details>

```{r fig5b-attn-rank, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: attention_weights_{tag}.tsv — produced by 04_interpret_attention.py
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis"
# Population: all test transcripts (up to 5 ORFs each)

attn_with_class <- attn_weights %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, label), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(label == 1, "NMD", "Control"))

p_attn <- attn_with_class %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(orf_rank), y = attn_weight, fill = class)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Attention Weights by ORF Rank (n=%s transcripts)", format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Rank 0 = priority ORF (ref CDS > SQANTI CDS > Kozak).",
       x = "ORF rank", y = "Attention weight", fill = NULL)

save_fig(p_attn, "fig4b_attention_by_rank")
```

```{r fig5c-max-attn-density, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: attention_weights_{tag}.tsv — produced by 04_interpret_attention.py
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis"
# Population: all test transcripts. 10 isoforms with all-zero weights (no valid ORFs) excluded.

max_attn_per_iso <- attn_with_class %>%
  group_by(isoform_id, class) %>%
  summarise(max_weight = max(attn_weight), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(max_weight > 0)  # exclude all-zero edge cases (no valid ORFs)

n_max_attn <- nrow(max_attn_per_iso)
n_nmd_max  <- sum(max_attn_per_iso$class == "NMD")
n_ctrl_max <- sum(max_attn_per_iso$class == "Control")

p_max_attn <- max_attn_per_iso %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = max_weight, fill = class)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Distribution of Top ORF Attention Weight (n=%s NMD, %s Control)",
                       format(n_nmd_max, big.mark = ","),
                       format(n_ctrl_max, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Max attention weight per isoform. Higher = model concentrates on a single dominant ORF.",
       x = "Max attention weight", y = "Density", fill = NULL)

save_fig(p_max_attn, "fig5c_max_attn_density")
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **Max attention weight:** The largest softmax attention weight across the 5 candidate ORFs for each isoform. A value of 1.0 means all attention is on a single ORF; 0.2 would mean perfectly uniform attention across 5 ORFs.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The median max attention weight is `r round(median(max_attn_per_iso$max_weight[max_attn_per_iso$class == "NMD"]), 3)` for NMD and `r round(median(max_attn_per_iso$max_weight[max_attn_per_iso$class == "Control"]), 3)` for Control. Both classes show strong single-ORF dominance, with most isoforms concentrating >50% of attention on one ORF.
- **(Data)** NMD isoforms have slightly lower max attention weights than Controls, consistent with the higher attention entropy observed above — the model is less certain about ORF selection for NMD transcripts.

<details><summary>Table 5b: ORF composition by rank and NMD status</summary>

```{r table4b-orf-composition}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: selected_orfs.tsv — produced by data_prep.py::select_priority_orfs()
# Methods: METHODS.md § "v2 Priority ORF Selection"
# Population: test-set ORFs at ranks 0 and 1

rank01_comp <- test_orfs %>%
  filter(orf_rank %in% c(0, 1)) %>%
  count(nmd_status, orf_rank, selection_reason) %>%
  group_by(nmd_status, orf_rank) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>% ungroup()

render_dt(rank01_comp,
          caption = "Table 5b: ORF selection reason by rank and NMD status (ref_cds / sqanti_cds / kozak)")
```

</details>

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The model strongly favors a **dominant ORF**: rank 0 is the most-attended ORF in `r ent_stats$pct_rank0_dominant[ent_stats$class == "NMD"]`% of NMD transcripts and `r ent_stats$pct_rank0_dominant[ent_stats$class == "Control"]`% of controls.
- **(Data)** Entropy is similar between classes (NMD: `r ent_stats$mean_entropy[ent_stats$class == "NMD"]` bits, Control: `r ent_stats$mean_entropy[ent_stats$class == "Control"]` bits).
- **(Hypothesis)** The low entropy and rank-0 dominance suggest the model treats NMD prediction primarily as a single-ORF problem, rather than integrating signals across multiple ORFs.


---

# 6. Subgroup-Specific Analysis {#sec6-subgroups}

This section stratifies the model's behavior by NMD subgroup: **PTC+** (canonical EJC-dependent), **PTC-, ref ATG retained** (non-canonical, reference reading frame present but no downstream EJC), and **PTC-, ref ATG lost** (reference ATG unavailable). Subgroup definitions and structural characteristics are presented first, followed by subgroup-specific visualization of start site, stop site, and attention signals.

## 6.1 Isoform Subgroup Definitions {#sec6-definitions}

We stratify all test-set transcripts into four subgroups: one Control and three NMD subgroups based on PTC status and reference CDS availability. These groups are used consistently throughout Section 6.

```{r sec6-subgroup-definitions}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: predictions_{tag}.tsv, selected_orfs.tsv, ref_cds_features.tsv, attention_entropy_{tag}.tsv
# Population: all test-set transcripts (n = n_test)

# Build a single analysis data frame with subgroup assignments for all test transcripts
# Subgroups based on the upstream category field from 05t_ref_cds_features.R:
#   effectively_ptc       → NMD PTC+
#   no_downstream_ejc     → NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained
#   truncated_no_ejc      → NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained
#   ref_atg_lost          → NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost
#   no_ref_isoform        → NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost
#   not_atg_in_target     → NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost
#   no_stop_in_target     → NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost

sec8_data <- test_rank0 %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, outcome), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(ref_features %>%
              select(isoform_id, ref_atg_available, ref_downstream_ejc, category,
                     ref_utr5_length, ref_utr5_orf_coverage,
                     ref_uorf_count_outframe, ref_uorf_count_overlapping),
            by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(td2_features %>%
              select(isoform_id, td2_downstream_ejc, td2_log_utr3_length,
                     td2_utr5_orf_coverage, td2_uorf_count_outframe,
                     td2_uorf_count_overlapping, td2_utr5_length),
            by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(attn_entropy %>% select(isoform_id, attn_entropy, argmax_orf),
            by = "isoform_id") %>%
  mutate(subgroup = case_when(
    label == 0 ~ "Control",
    # PTC+ by ref CDS
    label == 1 & category == "effectively_ptc" ~ "NMD PTC+",
    # Ref ATG lost but PTC+ by TD2 → still PTC+
    label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                  "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") &
      !is.na(td2_downstream_ejc) & td2_downstream_ejc > 0 ~ "NMD PTC+",
    # Ref ATG retained, no PTC
    label == 1 & category %in% c("no_downstream_ejc", "truncated_no_ejc") ~
      "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained",
    # Ref ATG lost AND no PTC by TD2
    label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                  "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") ~
      "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost",
    TRUE ~ "NMD other"
  ))

subgroup_order <- c("Control", "NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained",
                    "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost")
sec8_data$subgroup <- factor(sec8_data$subgroup, levels = subgroup_order)
stopifnot("Unclassified transcripts detected" = !any(is.na(sec8_data$subgroup)))

subgroup_pal <- c("Control" = "#67a9cf", "NMD PTC+" = "#ef8a62",
                  "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained" = "#d95f02",
                  "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost" = "#8c510a")
```

```{r sec6-derived-vars, include=FALSE}
# Derived variables used by multiple Section 6 subsections
nmd_subgroups <- sec8_data %>% filter(label == 1)
n_nmd_sec8 <- nrow(nmd_subgroups)

# Performance by subgroup (used in summary and Section 7.2)
perf_by_subgroup <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            n_correct = sum(outcome == "TP"),
            sensitivity = mean(outcome == "TP"),
            mean_prob = mean(prob),
            median_prob = median(prob),
            mean_entropy = mean(attn_entropy, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")
```

```{r fig6a-subgroup-counts, fig.width=9, fig.height=4}
subgroup_counts <- sec8_data %>%
  count(subgroup) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

p_subgroups <- subgroup_counts %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = n, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)", format(n, big.mark = ","), pct)),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title = sprintf("A. Test Set Isoform Subgroups (n=%s)", format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "NMD transcripts stratified by PTC status: downstream EJC from reference CDS frame.",
       x = NULL, y = "Number of transcripts") +
  guides(fill = "none")

save_fig(p_subgroups, "fig6a_subgroup_counts", width = 9, height = 4)
```

**Subgroup definitions** (applied to all `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts, based on the `category` field from the upstream reference-CDS tracing in `05t_ref_cds_features.R`):

| Subgroup | Definition |
|----------|------------|
| **Control** | Non-NMD transcripts (label = 0) |
| **NMD PTC+** | PTC present by the best available CDS: ref CDS has >=1 downstream EJC (`effectively_ptc`), OR ref ATG is lost but TD2 CDS has >=1 downstream EJC. |
| **NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained** | Ref ATG available but reading frame terminates without downstream EJCs (`no_downstream_ejc`, `truncated_no_ejc`). |
| **NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost** | Ref ATG unavailable AND TD2 CDS also shows 0 downstream EJCs. PTC-negative by both reading frame definitions. |

- **PTC status** is defined by the best available CDS: first by the reference CDS frame (`ref_downstream_ejc > 0`), then by the TD2 CDS frame (`td2_downstream_ejc > 0`) for transcripts where the reference ATG is lost. This reclassifies ~47% of the original ref-ATG-lost group as PTC+ (they have PTCs from the TD2 reading frame).
- The **ref ATG lost, PTC-** group has zero-filled ref-CDS features but valid TD2 features. These transcripts lack PTCs by both reading frame definitions and represent the most clearly non-canonical NMD subpopulation.

<details><summary>Table 6a: Subgroup counts</summary>
```{r table7a-subgroup-counts}
render_dt(subgroup_counts, caption = "Table 6a: Test set isoform subgroups")
```
</details>

## 6.2 Structural Characteristics Differ by Subgroup {#sec6-structure}

The NMD PTC- subgroups differ from both NMD PTC+ and Control transcripts in 5'UTR structural properties. For subgroups with ref-CDS features (Control, PTC+, PTC- ref ATG retained), we use `ref_utr5_orf_coverage`; for the ref ATG lost subgroup, we use the TD2 equivalent (`td2_utr5_orf_coverage`).

```{r fig6b-utr5-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: ref_cds_features.tsv + td2_features.tsv joined to subgroup assignments
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Transcript-level features" and "TD2 features"
# Population: all test transcripts with non-missing 5'UTR coverage from either source

# Use ref features for Control/PTC+/PTC-, TD2 features for ref-ATG-lost
sec8_utr5 <- sec8_data %>%
  mutate(
    utr5_cov = case_when(
      subgroup %in% c("Control", "NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained") ~ ref_utr5_orf_coverage,
      subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost" ~ td2_utr5_orf_coverage
    ),
    utr5_len = case_when(
      subgroup %in% c("Control", "NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained") ~ ref_utr5_length,
      subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost" ~ td2_utr5_length
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(utr5_cov))

# Per-subgroup sample sizes for labels
utr5_n <- sec8_utr5 %>% count(subgroup)
utr5_labels <- setNames(
  sprintf("%s\n(n=%s)", utr5_n$subgroup, format(utr5_n$n, big.mark = ",")),
  utr5_n$subgroup)
sec8_utr5$subgroup_label <- utr5_labels[as.character(sec8_utr5$subgroup)]
sec8_utr5$subgroup_label <- factor(sec8_utr5$subgroup_label,
  levels = utr5_labels[intersect(subgroup_order, utr5_n$subgroup)])

n_utr5 <- nrow(sec8_utr5)

# Panel B: 5'UTR length (all 4 subgroups — ref-CDS for Control/PTC+/PTC-, TD2 for ref-ATG-lost)
sec8_utr5_len <- sec8_utr5 %>% filter(!is.na(utr5_len))
n_utr5_len <- nrow(sec8_utr5_len)

# Rebuild labels for the length subset (may differ from coverage subset)
utr5_len_n <- sec8_utr5_len %>% count(subgroup)
utr5_len_labels <- setNames(
  sprintf("%s\n(n=%s)", utr5_len_n$subgroup, format(utr5_len_n$n, big.mark = ",")),
  utr5_len_n$subgroup)
sec8_utr5_len$subgroup_label <- utr5_len_labels[as.character(sec8_utr5_len$subgroup)]
sec8_utr5_len$subgroup_label <- factor(sec8_utr5_len$subgroup_label,
  levels = utr5_len_labels[intersect(subgroup_order, utr5_len_n$subgroup)])

p_utr5_len <- sec8_utr5_len %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup_label, y = utr5_len, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("B. 5'UTR Length by Subgroup (n=%s)", format(n_utr5_len, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "ref-CDS 5'UTR for Control/PTC+/PTC-; TD2 5'UTR for ref-ATG-lost.",
       x = NULL, y = "5'UTR length (nt)") +
  guides(fill = "none")

# Panel C: 5'UTR ORF coverage (all 4 subgroups — TD2 coverage used for ref-ATG-lost)
p_utr5_cov <- sec8_utr5 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup_label, y = utr5_cov, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("C. 5'UTR ORF Coverage by Subgroup (n=%s)", format(n_utr5, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "ref-CDS coverage for Control/PTC+/PTC-; TD2 coverage for ref-ATG-lost.",
       x = NULL, y = "5'UTR ORF coverage\n(fraction of 5'UTR occupied by uORFs)") +
  guides(fill = "none")

save_fig(p_utr5_len / p_utr5_cov, "fig6b_utr5_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 8)
```

```{r sec6-utr5-tests, include=FALSE}
# Pairwise Wilcoxon tests for 5'UTR features
ptcneg_retained <- "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"
ptcneg_lost <- "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"

wt_len_ptcneg_ctrl <- wilcox.test(utr5_len ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5_len %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("Control", ptcneg_retained)))
wt_len_ptcneg_ptcpos <- wilcox.test(utr5_len ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5_len %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("NMD PTC+", ptcneg_retained)))
wt_len_lost_ctrl <- wilcox.test(utr5_len ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5_len %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("Control", ptcneg_lost)))

wt_cov_ptcneg_ctrl <- wilcox.test(utr5_cov ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5 %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("Control", ptcneg_retained)))
wt_cov_ptcneg_ptcpos <- wilcox.test(utr5_cov ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5 %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("NMD PTC+", ptcneg_retained)))
wt_cov_ptcpos_ctrl <- wilcox.test(utr5_cov ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5 %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("Control", "NMD PTC+")))
wt_cov_lost_ctrl <- wilcox.test(utr5_cov ~ subgroup,
  data = sec8_utr5 %>% filter(subgroup %in% c("Control", ptcneg_lost)))

utr5_medians <- sec8_utr5 %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(med_utr5_len = round(median(utr5_len, na.rm = TRUE)),
            med_utr5_cov = round(median(utr5_cov, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            med_upstream_atgs = median(n_upstream_atgs, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **5'UTR length:** From transcript 5' end to CDS start. For Control/PTC+/PTC-: from the reference CDS frame. For NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost: from the TD2 CDS frame.
- **5'UTR ORF coverage:** Fraction of the 5'UTR occupied by upstream ORFs. For Control/PTC+/PTC-: computed from the reference CDS frame. For NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost: computed from the TD2 CDS frame.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** **NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained** transcripts have longer 5'UTRs (median `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_len[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"]` nt) than both NMD PTC+ (`r utr5_medians$med_utr5_len[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"]` nt, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_len_ptcneg_ptcpos$p.value)`) and Control (`r utr5_medians$med_utr5_len[utr5_medians$subgroup == "Control"]` nt, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_len_ptcneg_ctrl$p.value)`).
- **(Data)** 5'UTR ORF coverage is dramatically elevated in PTC-, ref ATG retained (median `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_cov[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"]`) vs NMD PTC+ (`r utr5_medians$med_utr5_cov[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"]`, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_cov_ptcneg_ptcpos$p.value)`) and Control (`r utr5_medians$med_utr5_cov[utr5_medians$subgroup == "Control"]`, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_cov_ptcneg_ctrl$p.value)`). NMD PTC+ and Control are similar (p = `r sprintf("%.2f", wt_cov_ptcpos_ctrl$p.value)`).
- **(Data)** The **NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost** group (using TD2-derived features) also shows elevated 5'UTR length (median `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_len[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"]` nt vs Control `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_len[utr5_medians$subgroup == "Control"]` nt, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_len_lost_ctrl$p.value)`) and ORF coverage (median `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_cov[utr5_medians$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"]` vs Control `r utr5_medians$med_utr5_cov[utr5_medians$subgroup == "Control"]`, p = `r sprintf("%.1e", wt_cov_lost_ctrl$p.value)`).
- **(Data)** The 5'UTR complexity difference is specific to non-canonical NMD subgroups: among PTC+ transcripts, NMD and Control 5'UTRs are indistinguishable.

## 6.3 Input Importance by Subgroup {#sec6-input-importance}

This section shows how the model's reliance on each input type varies across NMD subgroups: structural ORF features, ATG branch sequence, and stop branch sequence.

```{r fig6-struct-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, eval=file.exists(file.path(results_dir, paste0("sample_shap_structural_", best_tag, ".tsv")))}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: sample_shap_structural_{tag}.tsv — per-sample DeepSHAP (5 ORF features)
# Population: all test transcripts across 4 subgroups

shap_struct_sg <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("sample_shap_structural_", best_tag, ".tsv"))) %>%
  inner_join(sec8_data %>% select(isoform_id, subgroup), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  filter(label > 0.5, subgroup != "NMD other")

feat_labels <- c(n_downstream_ejc = "Downstream EJC count",
                  is_ref_cds = "Is reference CDS",
                  is_sqanti_cds = "Is SQANTI/TD2 CDS",
                  frac_start = "Fractional start",
                  frac_stop = "Fractional stop")

struct_sg_summary <- shap_struct_sg %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(frac_start, frac_stop, is_ref_cds, is_sqanti_cds, n_downstream_ejc),
               names_to = "feature", values_to = "shap_val") %>%
  group_by(subgroup, feature) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs = mean(abs(shap_val)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(feature_label = feat_labels[feature],
         feature_label = factor(feature_label, levels = rev(feat_labels)))

p_struct_sg <- struct_sg_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = feature_label, fill = mean_abs)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_abs)), size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#d95f02", name = "Mean |SHAP|") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top", labels = function(x) stringr::str_wrap(x, width = 14)) +
  labs(title = "D. Structural Feature Importance by NMD Subgroup",
       subtitle = "Per-sample DeepSHAP, 500 background, 5 replicates. NMD transcripts only.",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10))

save_fig(p_struct_sg, "fig6_struct_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 5)
```

```{r fig6-atg-profile-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: shap_profile_atg_subgroup_joint_{tag}.tsv — per-position total nuc |SHAP| by subgroup
# Population: all test transcripts from joint DeepSHAP run 1

atg_sg_profile_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("shap_profile_atg_subgroup_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
stop_sg_profile_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("shap_profile_stop_subgroup_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_sg_profiles <- file.exists(atg_sg_profile_path) && file.exists(stop_sg_profile_path)

if (has_sg_profiles) {
  atg_sg_prof <- read.delim(atg_sg_profile_path) %>%
    filter(subgroup != "NMD other") %>%
    mutate(subgroup = factor(subgroup, levels = names(subgroup_pal_short)))
  stop_sg_prof <- read.delim(stop_sg_profile_path) %>%
    filter(subgroup != "NMD other") %>%
    mutate(subgroup = factor(subgroup, levels = names(subgroup_pal_short)))

  p_atg_sg <- atg_sg_prof %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = total_nuc_abs_shap, color = subgroup)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.15, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
    annotate("text", x = -100, y = Inf, label = "5'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
    annotate("text", x = 100, y = Inf, label = "ORF body", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
    labs(title = "E. ATG Branch: Nucleotide |SHAP| by Subgroup",
         subtitle = "Loess smooth. Total A+C+G+T |SHAP| per position. Dashed = ATG.",
         x = "Position relative to ATG", y = "Total nucleotide |SHAP|", color = NULL) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

  save_fig(p_atg_sg, "fig6_atg_profile_subgroup", width = 10, height = 5)
}
```

```{r fig6-stop-profile-subgroup, eval=has_sg_profiles, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
p_stop_sg <- stop_sg_prof %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = total_nuc_abs_shap, color = subgroup)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.15, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
  annotate("text", x = -150, y = Inf, label = "ORF body", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = 150, y = Inf, label = "3'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
  labs(title = "F. Stop Branch: Nucleotide |SHAP| by Subgroup",
       subtitle = "Loess smooth. Total A+C+G+T |SHAP| per position. Dashed = stop codon.",
       x = "Position relative to stop codon", y = "Total nucleotide |SHAP|", color = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

save_fig(p_stop_sg, "fig6_stop_profile_subgroup", width = 10, height = 5)
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** **Structural features:** `n_downstream_ejc` dominates for PTC+ but drops sharply for PTC- ref ATG retained (which by definition lacks downstream EJCs). `is_ref_cds` is important for PTC+ and PTC- ref ATG retained (where the reference CDS frame is still available) but zero for ref ATG lost.
- **(Data)** **ATG branch:** PTC- ref ATG retained shows elevated |SHAP| compared to Control and PTC+, consistent with the KernelSHAP finding that this subgroup relies more heavily on start codon context (27% ATG branch vs 8% for PTC+).
- **(Data)** **Stop branch:** PTC+ shows dramatically higher |SHAP| than all other subgroups, with a pronounced spike at the stop codon and elevated 3'UTR signal — reflecting the dominant EJC/junction signature. PTC- ref ATG retained shows minimal stop branch signal, confirming the model's alternative prediction strategy for this subgroup.

## 6.4 Start Site Signals by Subgroup {#sec6-start-subgroup}

```{r fig6-atg-subgroup, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: motif_logo_atg_subgroup_joint_{tag}.tsv — joint DeepSHAP, all test, 5-run avg
# Population: all test transcripts stratified by subgroup

atg_sg_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logo_atg_subgroup_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_atg_sg <- file.exists(atg_sg_path)

if (has_atg_sg) {
  atg_sg <- read.delim(atg_sg_path) %>%
    filter(relative_position >= -11, relative_position <= 11,
           subgroup != "NMD other")

  positions <- sort(unique(atg_sg$relative_position))
  # Kozak numbering (same as Section 3.1)
  kozak_num <- ifelse(positions >= -1, positions + 2, positions + 1)
  pos_labels <- ifelse(kozak_num > 0, paste0("+", kozak_num), as.character(kozak_num))
  pos_labels[positions == -1] <- "A(+1)"
  pos_labels[positions == 0]  <- "T(+2)"
  pos_labels[positions == 1]  <- "G(+3)"

  # Build per-subgroup logo matrices and plot
  sg_plots <- list()
  for (sg in c("NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost", "Control")) {
    sg_data <- atg_sg %>% filter(subgroup == sg)
    n_sg <- sg_data$n_samples[1]
    mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(positions),
                  dimnames = list(c("A","C","G","T"), positions))
    for (i in seq_along(positions)) {
      for (j in 1:4) {
        v <- sg_data$mean_contrib[sg_data$relative_position == positions[i] &
                                   sg_data$channel == c("A","C","G","T")[j]]
        if (length(v) == 1) mat[j, i] <- v
      }
    }

    sg_plots[[sg]] <- ggseqlogo(mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(positions), labels = pos_labels) +
      annotate("rect", xmin = which(positions == -1) - 0.5,
               xmax = which(positions == 1) + 0.5,
               ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
      labs(title = sprintf("%s (n=%s)", sg, format(n_sg, big.mark = ",")),
           x = NULL, y = "SHAP × input") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
            plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
  }

  save_fig(
    (sg_plots[[1]] + sg_plots[[2]]) / (sg_plots[[3]] + sg_plots[[4]]),
    "fig6_atg_subgroup", width = 12, height = 10)
}
```

```{r sec6-kozak-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: selected_orfs.tsv (rank-0 ORFs) + SQANTI-corrected FASTA
# Kozak scores: ORFik kozak_score (0-2) and Isopair scoreKozakPWM (log-odds PWM)

library(Biostrings)

fasta_path <- "/Users/petecastaldi/claude_projects/nmd/sqanti/nmd_lungcells/results/nmd_lungcells_corrected.fasta"
has_fasta <- file.exists(fasta_path)

if (has_fasta) {
  sel_orfs <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "selected_orfs.tsv")) %>%
    filter(orf_rank == 0, isoform_id %in% preds$isoform_id)

  # Assign subgroups
  kozak_data <- sel_orfs %>%
    left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, label), by = "isoform_id") %>%
    left_join(ref_features %>% select(isoform_id, category), by = "isoform_id") %>%
    left_join(td2_features %>% select(isoform_id, td2_downstream_ejc), by = "isoform_id") %>%
    mutate(subgroup = case_when(
      label == 0 ~ "Control",
      label == 1 & category == "effectively_ptc" ~ "NMD PTC+",
      label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                    "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") &
        !is.na(td2_downstream_ejc) & td2_downstream_ejc > 0 ~ "NMD PTC+",
      label == 1 & category %in% c("no_downstream_ejc", "truncated_no_ejc") ~
        "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained",
      label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                    "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") ~
        "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost",
      TRUE ~ "NMD other"
    )) %>%
    filter(subgroup != "NMD other") %>%
    mutate(subgroup = factor(subgroup, levels = names(subgroup_pal_short)))

  # Score with Isopair KozakPWM
  tx_seqs <- readDNAStringSet(fasta_path)
  names(tx_seqs) <- sub(" .*", "", names(tx_seqs))
  kozak_data$seq <- as.character(tx_seqs[kozak_data$isoform_id])
  kozak_pwm <- Isopair::scoreKozakPWM(kozak_data$seq, kozak_data$orf_start)
  kozak_data$pwm_score <- kozak_pwm$score
  rm(tx_seqs); gc(verbose = FALSE)

  # Isopair KozakPWM by subgroup
  p_pwm <- kozak_data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = pwm_score, fill = subgroup)) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.7, scale = "width") +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, fill = "white", alpha = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.3) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 3) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
    labs(title = "Kozak PWM Score at Rank-0 ORF ATG by Subgroup",
         subtitle = "Isopair scoreKozakPWM: log-odds across 8 positions (-6 to +5). Diamond = mean. Dashed = neutral.",
         x = NULL, y = "PWM score (log-odds)") +
    guides(fill = "none") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

  save_fig(p_pwm, "fig6_kozak_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 5)
}
```

```{r tbl6-kozak, eval=has_fasta}
# Summary table with both Kozak measures and selection reason breakdown
kozak_summary <- kozak_data %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean_orfik = round(mean(kozak_score, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            pct_strong_orfik = round(100 * mean(kozak_score == 2, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
            mean_pwm = round(mean(pwm_score, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            median_pwm = round(median(pwm_score, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            pct_ref_cds = round(100 * mean(selection_reason == "ref_cds"), 1),
            pct_sqanti = round(100 * mean(selection_reason == "sqanti_cds"), 1),
            pct_kozak_fill = round(100 * mean(selection_reason == "kozak"), 1),
            .groups = "drop")
render_dt(kozak_summary,
          caption = "Kozak strength at rank-0 ORF by subgroup (ORFik + Isopair PWM)")
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **Kozak PWM score:** Log-odds score from `Isopair::scoreKozakPWM`, which evaluates the sequence context at 8 positions spanning -6 to +5 relative to the A of the ATG start codon. The position weight matrix (PWM) is derived from vertebrate translation initiation site frequencies. Positive scores indicate favorable initiation context; zero is neutral; negative scores indicate unfavorable context. The PWM captures contributions from all 8 positions simultaneously, providing a more nuanced measure than the binary ORFik score (which only checks purine at -3 and G at +4).
- **Rank-0 ORF:** The priority-selected ORF for each transcript (see Section 0). For PTC+ and PTC- ref ATG retained, this is the **reference CDS ORF** (100%). For PTC- ref ATG lost, this is predominantly the **SQANTI/TD2 CDS ORF** (`r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$pct_sqanti[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"], 0)`%), since the reference ATG is unavailable.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** The Kozak motif signal at position -3 (the canonical R position) is strongest in PTC+, consistent with the model recognizing well-conserved CDS start codons. PTC- ref ATG retained shows weaker and more diffuse start codon context signals.
- **(Data)** The Kozak PWM confirms the subgroup hierarchy: **NMD PTC+** has the strongest initiation context (mean PWM = `r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"], 2)`), followed by **Control** (`r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "Control"], 2)`), then **PTC- ref ATG retained** (`r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"], 2)`), and finally **PTC- ref ATG lost** (`r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"], 2)`).
- **(Data)** The weak Kozak in **PTC- ref ATG lost** reflects the ORF type, not NMD biology: these rank-0 ORFs are predominantly SQANTI/TD2 CDS calls (the reference ATG is unavailable), and TD2-selected ORFs have weak Kozak across the board — Control SQANTI ORFs show similarly low PWM scores. The SHAP motif logos (above) may show intermediate signal for this group because they reflect the model's *use* of whatever Kozak context is present, not the absolute Kozak strength.
- **(Interpretation)** The weak start site signal in **PTC- ref ATG retained** is the more biologically interesting finding. These rank-0 ORFs *are* the reference CDS (100%), yet their Kozak context is weaker than PTC+ reference CDS ORFs (PWM `r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"], 2)` vs `r if(has_fasta) round(kozak_summary$mean_pwm[kozak_summary$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"], 2)`). The reference reading frame does not produce a PTC with downstream EJCs, so the model cannot rely on canonical start-site features. The elevated ATG branch importance for this subgroup (Section 6.7) likely reflects the model attending to the broader 5'UTR landscape rather than the Kozak motif itself.

## 6.5 Stop Site Signals by Subgroup {#sec6-stop-subgroup}

```{r fig6-stop-subgroup, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: motif_logo_stop_subgroup_joint_{tag}.tsv — joint DeepSHAP, all test, 5-run avg

stop_sg_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("motif_logo_stop_subgroup_joint_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_stop_sg <- file.exists(stop_sg_path)

if (has_stop_sg) {
  stop_sg <- read.delim(stop_sg_path) %>%
    filter(relative_position >= -11, relative_position <= 11,
           subgroup != "NMD other")

  positions <- sort(unique(stop_sg$relative_position))
  stop_num <- ifelse(positions >= -1, positions + 2, positions + 1)
  stop_labels <- ifelse(stop_num > 0, paste0("+", stop_num), as.character(stop_num))
  stop_labels[positions == -1] <- "T(+1)"
  stop_labels[positions == 0]  <- "+2"
  stop_labels[positions == 1]  <- "+3"

  sg_plots_stop <- list()
  for (sg in c("NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost", "Control")) {
    sg_data <- stop_sg %>% filter(subgroup == sg)
    n_sg <- sg_data$n_samples[1]
    mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 4, ncol = length(positions),
                  dimnames = list(c("A","C","G","T"), positions))
    for (i in seq_along(positions)) {
      for (j in 1:4) {
        v <- sg_data$mean_contrib[sg_data$relative_position == positions[i] &
                                   sg_data$channel == c("A","C","G","T")[j]]
        if (length(v) == 1) mat[j, i] <- v
      }
    }

    sg_plots_stop[[sg]] <- ggseqlogo(mat, method = "custom", seq_type = "dna") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(positions), labels = stop_labels) +
      annotate("rect", xmin = which(positions == -1) - 0.5,
               xmax = which(positions == 1) + 0.5,
               ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.3) +
      labs(title = sprintf("%s (n=%s)", sg, format(n_sg, big.mark = ",")),
           x = NULL, y = "SHAP × input") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
            plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
  }

  save_fig(
    (sg_plots_stop[[1]] + sg_plots_stop[[2]]) / (sg_plots_stop[[3]] + sg_plots_stop[[4]]),
    "fig6_stop_subgroup", width = 12, height = 10)
}
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** PTC+ shows the strongest stop codon SHAP signal, with clear TGA preference (G positive at position +2). The downstream 3'UTR positions show junction-related signals.
- **(Data)** PTC- ref ATG retained shows near-zero stop codon SHAP, consistent with the absence of downstream EJCs in this subgroup — the stop branch contributes minimally to their NMD prediction.
- **(Data)** PTC- ref ATG lost shows intermediate stop codon signals, reflecting the mixed PTC status of this group under alternative reading frames.

## 6.6 Feature Attribution Differs by Subgroup {#sec6-attribution}

The model uses different ORF-level features for different subgroups, as measured by per-sample DeepSHAP attribution of the 5 ORF structural features (frac_start, frac_stop, is_ref_cds, is_sqanti_cds, n_downstream_ejc). SHAP values were computed for all `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts using 500 background samples, averaged across 5 replicates with different background random seeds.

```{r fig6c-attribution-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=7, eval=file.exists(file.path(results_dir, paste0("sample_shap_structural_", best_tag, ".tsv")))}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: sample_shap_structural_{tag}.tsv — per-sample DeepSHAP (5 ORF features)
#   Produced by deepshap.py --branches structural --n-explain 0 --n-background 500
#   Averaged across 5 replicates (different background seeds). All test transcripts.
# Population: all test transcripts across 4 subgroups

shap_orf_samples <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, paste0("sample_shap_structural_", best_tag, ".tsv")))

# Join subgroup assignments
shap_with_subgroup <- shap_orf_samples %>%
  inner_join(sec8_data %>% select(isoform_id, subgroup), by = "isoform_id")

n_attr <- nrow(shap_with_subgroup)

# Compute mean |SHAP| per feature per subgroup
orf_feat_cols <- c("frac_start", "frac_stop", "is_ref_cds", "is_sqanti_cds", "n_downstream_ejc")
attr_summary <- shap_with_subgroup %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(orf_feat_cols), names_to = "feature", values_to = "shap_val") %>%
  group_by(subgroup, feature) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_shap = mean(abs(shap_val)),
            mean_shap = mean(shap_val),
            n = n(),
            .groups = "drop")

# Readable feature labels
feature_labels_v5 <- c(
  n_downstream_ejc = "Downstream EJC count",
  is_ref_cds = "Is reference CDS",
  is_sqanti_cds = "Is SQANTI/TD2 CDS",
  frac_start = "Fractional start position",
  frac_stop = "Fractional stop position"
)
attr_summary$feature_label <- feature_labels_v5[attr_summary$feature]

# Order by importance in PTC+ subgroup
feat_order <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC+") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_abs_shap)) %>%
  pull(feature)
attr_summary$feature_label <- factor(attr_summary$feature_label,
  levels = rev(feature_labels_v5[feat_order]))

# Panel G: Heatmap of mean |SHAP| (NMD subgroups only)
p_attr_abs <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup != "Control") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = feature_label, fill = mean_abs_shap)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_abs_shap)), size = 5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#d95f02", name = "Mean |SHAP|") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(title = sprintf("G. ORF Feature Importance by NMD Subgroup (n=%s NMD transcripts)",
                       format(sum(shap_with_subgroup$subgroup != "Control"), big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "DeepSHAP, 500 background, mean of 5 replicates. Brighter = more important.",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.x.top = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12))

save_fig(p_attr_abs, "fig6c_attribution_heatmap", width = 10, height = 5)

# Panel H: Heatmap of signed SHAP (diverging red/blue)
p_attr_signed <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup != "Control") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = feature_label, fill = mean_shap)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_shap)), size = 5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b",
                       midpoint = 0, name = "Mean SHAP\n(signed)") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(title = "H. Signed ORF Feature Attribution by Subgroup",
       subtitle = "Red = pushes toward NMD, blue = pushes away.",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.x.top = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12))

save_fig(p_attr_signed, "fig6c_attribution_signed_heatmap", width = 10, height = 5)
```

```{r sec6-attribution-stats, include=FALSE, eval=exists("attr_summary")}
# Extract key values for inline text
shap_ejc_ptcpos <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC+", feature == "n_downstream_ejc") %>% pull(mean_abs_shap)
shap_ejc_ptcneg <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained", feature == "n_downstream_ejc") %>% pull(mean_abs_shap)
shap_ejc_noref <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost", feature == "n_downstream_ejc") %>% pull(mean_abs_shap)
shap_ref_ptcpos <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC+", feature == "is_ref_cds") %>% pull(mean_abs_shap)
shap_ref_noref <- attr_summary %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost", feature == "is_ref_cds") %>% pull(mean_abs_shap)
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **DeepSHAP:** Per-sample SHAP values for each ORF structural feature, computed via `shap.DeepExplainer` with 500 background training samples. Values are in z-score normalized feature space. Averaged across 5 independent replicates (different background random seeds) for stability.
- **Panel G (magnitude heatmap):** Mean |SHAP| — how much each feature contributes to predictions regardless of direction. Brighter = more important.
- **Panel H (signed heatmap):** Mean signed SHAP — red = pushes toward NMD, blue = pushes away from NMD.

**Zero-fill note:** For the **NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost** subgroup, `is_ref_cds` is always 0 (no reference CDS ATG available). After z-score normalization, this zero becomes a constant negative z-score. Nonzero SHAP for `is_ref_cds` in the ref-ATG-lost column reflects the model responding to "this ORF is not the reference CDS" rather than to the absence of a reference frame per se.

**Why `is_ref_cds` has positive SHAP in ref ATG lost (Panel H):** This is the complement of the weak start site signal observed in Section 6.4. For **PTC- ref ATG retained**, `is_ref_cds = 1` and the signed SHAP is negative — the presence of the reference CDS actually pushes *away* from NMD, because the reference reading frame is intact and does not trigger canonical NMD. For **PTC- ref ATG lost**, `is_ref_cds = 0` always, so after z-normalization the feature takes a constant below-mean value. The positive SHAP reflects the model interpreting "this ORF is *not* the reference CDS" as evidence for NMD — which is biologically coherent, since loss of the reference reading frame is itself a hallmark of NMD-triggering isoforms. Together with the weak Kozak signal in Section 6.4, this reveals the model's learned logic: when the reference ATG is present but doesn't produce a PTC, the model must rely on alternative signals; when it is absent entirely, the absence itself is informative.

```{r sec6-attribution-obs, eval=exists("shap_ejc_ptcpos"), results='asis'}
cat("**Observations:**\n\n")
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** `n_downstream_ejc` is the dominant feature for **NMD PTC+** (%.3f), consistent with the canonical PTC + EJC mechanism. Its importance drops for **PTC- ref ATG retained** (%.3f) and **PTC- ref ATG lost** (%.3f).\n", shap_ejc_ptcpos, shap_ejc_ptcneg, shap_ejc_noref))
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** `is_ref_cds` importance is %.3f for PTC+ (confirming the model values ORFs that match the reference reading frame) but drops to %.3f for ref-ATG-lost (where it is always 0).\n", shap_ref_ptcpos, shap_ref_noref))
cat("- **(Data)** The positional features (`frac_start`, `frac_stop`) show modest importance across all subgroups, reflecting the model's use of ORF position within the transcript as a secondary signal.\n")
```

## 6.7 Branch Decomposition by Subgroup {#sec6-branch-subgroup}

```{r fig6-branch-subgroup, eval=has_kernel_shap, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: kernel_shap_branch_{tag}.tsv — exact Shapley values for 3 branches
# Population: all test transcripts, stratified by subgroup

ks_sg <- ks %>%
  inner_join(sec8_data %>% select(isoform_id, subgroup), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  filter(label > 0.5)  # NMD only

# Branch % by subgroup
ks_sg_summary <- ks_sg %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            atg_abs = mean(abs(shap_atg)),
            stop_abs = mean(abs(shap_stop)),
            struct_abs = mean(abs(shap_structural)),
            atg_signed = mean(shap_atg),
            stop_signed = mean(shap_stop),
            struct_signed = mean(shap_structural),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(total = atg_abs + stop_abs + struct_abs,
         pct_atg = round(100 * atg_abs / total, 1),
         pct_stop = round(100 * stop_abs / total, 1),
         pct_struct = round(100 * struct_abs / total, 1))

# Stacked bar chart of branch %
ks_sg_long <- ks_sg_summary %>%
  select(subgroup, pct_atg, pct_stop, pct_struct) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("pct_"), names_to = "branch", values_to = "pct") %>%
  mutate(branch = case_when(
    branch == "pct_atg" ~ "ATG (start codon)",
    branch == "pct_stop" ~ "Stop (3'UTR)",
    branch == "pct_struct" ~ "Structural (ORF features)"),
    branch = factor(branch, levels = c("Structural (ORF features)", "Stop (3'UTR)", "ATG (start codon)")))

p_branch_sg <- ks_sg_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = pct, fill = branch)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%", pct)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Structural (ORF features)" = "#d95f02",
                                "Stop (3'UTR)" = "#FF6B6B",
                                "ATG (start codon)" = "#4ECDC4")) +
  labs(title = "I. Branch Decomposition by NMD Subgroup (exact Shapley, KernelSHAP)",
       subtitle = "Percentage of total |SHAP| attributed to each model branch. Exact additivity (residual = 0).",
       x = NULL, y = "% of total |SHAP|", fill = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

save_fig(p_branch_sg, "fig6_branch_subgroup", width = 10, height = 6)
```

```{r tbl6-branch-subgroup, eval=has_kernel_shap}
render_dt(
  ks_sg_summary %>%
    select(subgroup, n, pct_struct, pct_stop, pct_atg, total,
           atg_signed, stop_signed, struct_signed) %>%
    mutate(across(c(total, atg_signed, stop_signed, struct_signed), ~round(., 4))),
  caption = "Table 6b: Branch decomposition by NMD subgroup (KernelSHAP)")
```

```{r sec6-branch-obs, eval=has_kernel_shap, results='asis'}
ptcpos <- ks_sg_summary %>% filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC+")
ptcneg_ret <- ks_sg_summary %>% filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained")
ptcneg_lost <- ks_sg_summary %>% filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost")

# Count ATG-dominated samples in PTC- ref ATG retained
atg_dom_ret <- ks_sg %>%
  filter(subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained") %>%
  mutate(dominant = case_when(
    abs(shap_atg) > abs(shap_stop) & abs(shap_atg) > abs(shap_structural) ~ "ATG",
    abs(shap_stop) > abs(shap_structural) ~ "Stop",
    TRUE ~ "Structural")) %>%
  filter(dominant == "ATG")

cat("**Observations:**\n\n")
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** **PTC+** is dominated by structural features (%s%%) and the stop branch (%s%%), consistent with the canonical PTC + EJC mechanism. The ATG branch contributes only %s%%.\n",
    ptcpos$pct_struct, ptcpos$pct_stop, ptcpos$pct_atg))
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** **PTC- ref ATG retained** shows a dramatically different profile: the ATG branch rises to **%s%%** (vs %s%% in PTC+), while structural features drop to %s%% with the stop branch at %s%%. In %d/%d (%s%%) of these isoforms, the ATG branch is the dominant contributor — the model relies on start codon context and 5'UTR composition when the canonical EJC pathway is absent.\n",
    ptcneg_ret$pct_atg, ptcpos$pct_atg, ptcneg_ret$pct_struct, ptcneg_ret$pct_stop,
    nrow(atg_dom_ret), ptcneg_ret$n, round(100*nrow(atg_dom_ret)/ptcneg_ret$n, 1)))
cat(sprintf("- **(Data)** **PTC- ref ATG lost** is intermediate: structural features (%s%%) still dominate (driven by the `is_ref_cds=0` signal), with the stop branch at %s%% and ATG at %s%%.\n",
    ptcneg_lost$pct_struct, ptcneg_lost$pct_stop, ptcneg_lost$pct_atg))
cat("- **(Interpretation)** The model has learned qualitatively different prediction strategies for each subgroup. PTC+ uses the canonical pathway (structural EJC features + junction sequence signal). PTC- ref ATG retained — the most challenging subgroup — shifts to the ATG branch, suggesting the model detects non-canonical NMD signals in the 5'UTR and start codon context. This is consistent with uORF-mediated and translational regulation mechanisms proposed in the NMD literature.\n")
```

## 6.8 Attention Patterns Differ by Subgroup {#sec6-attention-subgroup}

```{r sec6-attention-subgroup-setup, eval=has_subgroup_shap}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: attention_weights_{tag}.tsv joined to subgroup assignments from Section 6.1 logic
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis"
# Population: all test transcripts, classified into 4 subgroups.

# Assign subgroups using same logic as Section 7.1
attn_sg <- attn_weights %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, label), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(ref_features %>% select(isoform_id, category), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(td2_features %>% select(isoform_id, td2_downstream_ejc), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  left_join(all_orfs %>%
              filter(orf_rank == 0) %>%
              select(isoform_id, is_ref_cds, is_sqanti_cds, has_downstream_ejc),
            by = "isoform_id") %>%
  mutate(subgroup = case_when(
    label == 0 ~ "Control",
    label == 1 & category == "effectively_ptc" ~ "NMD PTC+",
    label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                  "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") &
      !is.na(td2_downstream_ejc) & td2_downstream_ejc > 0 ~ "NMD PTC+",
    label == 1 & category %in% c("no_downstream_ejc", "truncated_no_ejc") ~
      "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained",
    label == 1 & category %in% c("ref_atg_lost", "no_ref_isoform",
                                  "not_atg_in_target", "no_stop_in_target") ~
      "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost",
    TRUE ~ "NMD other"
  )) %>%
  filter(subgroup != "NMD other") %>%
  mutate(subgroup = factor(subgroup, levels = names(subgroup_pal_short)))
```

```{r fig6d-attn-subgroup, eval=has_subgroup_shap, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# Panel A: % rank-0 dominant by subgroup
rank0_dom <- attn_sg %>%
  filter(orf_rank == 0) %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(
    n_tx = n(),
    mean_attn = round(mean(attn_weight), 3),
    median_attn = round(median(attn_weight), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Compute % rank-0 dominant (max attention at rank 0)
max_attn <- attn_sg %>%
  group_by(isoform_id, subgroup) %>%
  slice_max(attn_weight, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(pct_rank0_dom = round(100 * mean(orf_rank == 0), 1),
            n_tx = n(), .groups = "drop")

p_rank0_dom <- ggplot(max_attn, aes(x = subgroup, y = pct_rank0_dom, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%\n(n=%s)", pct_rank0_dom, format(n_tx, big.mark = ","))),
            vjust = -0.2, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 105)) +
  labs(title = "J. Rank-0 ORF Dominance by Subgroup",
       subtitle = "% of transcripts where rank-0 ORF receives highest attention",
       x = NULL, y = "% rank-0 dominant") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

# Panel B: Mean attention by rank, faceted by subgroup
attn_by_rank_sg <- attn_sg %>%
  group_by(subgroup, orf_rank) %>%
  summarise(mean_attn = mean(attn_weight), .groups = "drop")

p_attn_rank_sg <- ggplot(attn_by_rank_sg, aes(x = factor(orf_rank), y = mean_attn,
                                                fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~subgroup, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
  labs(title = "K. Attention Distribution Across ORF Ranks by Subgroup",
       subtitle = "Mean attention weight per rank (all test transcripts)",
       x = "ORF rank", y = "Mean attention") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

save_fig(p_rank0_dom / p_attn_rank_sg, "fig6d_attention_subgroup", width = 10, height = 8)
```

```{r tbl6d-attn-subgroup, eval=has_subgroup_shap}
# Summary table with ORF type attention
orf_type_attn <- attn_sg %>%
  filter(orf_rank == 0) %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(
    n_tx = n(),
    mean_rank0_attn = round(mean(attn_weight), 3),
    median_rank0_attn = round(median(attn_weight), 3),
    pct_with_ref_cds = round(100 * mean(is_ref_cds == 1, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    mean_attn_ref_cds = round(mean(attn_weight[is_ref_cds == 1], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    mean_attn_sqanti_cds = round(mean(attn_weight[is_sqanti_cds == 1], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
render_dt(orf_type_attn,
          caption = "Table 6c: Attention on rank-0 ORF by subgroup and CDS type")
```

<details><summary>**Variable definitions** (click to expand)</summary>

- **pct_rank0_dom**: Percentage of transcripts where the rank-0 ORF receives the highest attention weight among all K=5 ORFs.
- **mean_rank0_attn**: Mean attention weight assigned to the rank-0 ORF.
- **pct_with_ref_cds**: Percentage of rank-0 ORFs that are flagged as reference CDS.
- **mean_attn_ref_cds**: Mean attention weight on rank-0 ORFs flagged as is_ref_cds = 1.
- **mean_attn_sqanti_cds**: Mean attention weight on rank-0 ORFs flagged as is_sqanti_cds = 1.

</details>

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** **PTC+** shows the most focused attention: rank-0 is dominant in ~95% of transcripts, with median attention ≈ 0.88. The model is highly confident about which ORF to evaluate.
- **(Data)** **PTC- ref ATG retained** has strikingly diffuse attention: rank-0 is dominant in only ~50% of transcripts. Attention "leaks" substantially to ranks 1-4, indicating the model searches across multiple ORFs for signals. This is the hardest subgroup — the model is genuinely uncertain which ORF explains NMD status.
- **(Data)** **PTC- ref ATG lost** falls in between (~63% rank-0 dominant), with attention shifting toward SQANTI/TD2 CDS ORFs when ref CDS is unavailable.
- **(Interpretation)** The attention mechanism reveals three distinct decision strategies: (1) PTC+: lock onto ref CDS with strong Kozak, check for downstream EJC; (2) PTC- ATG lost: shift to TD2/SQANTI CDS as fallback anchor; (3) PTC- ATG retained: distribute attention across multiple ORFs, searching for alternative explanations (upstream ORFs, out-of-frame ORFs). This is consistent with the subgroup-specific sequence-level findings in Section 6.3.

```{r fig6-shap-start-site, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: shap_profile_atg_subgroup_joint_{tag}.tsv — per-position total nuc |SHAP| by subgroup
# Population: all test transcripts from joint DeepSHAP run 1

# SHAP-based start site importance by subgroup
# Analogous to Panel K (attention by rank) but using positional DeepSHAP values
# to show where the model places importance around the ATG window

if (has_sg_profiles) {
  # Focus on the ±50bp region around ATG to highlight start site differences
  atg_sg_focus <- atg_sg_prof %>%
    filter(abs(relative_position) <= 50,
           subgroup %in% c("NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained",
                            "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"))

  p_shap_start <- atg_sg_focus %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = relative_position, y = total_nuc_abs_shap, color = subgroup)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.25, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1, linewidth = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
    annotate("text", x = -25, y = Inf, label = "5'UTR", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
    annotate("text", x = 25, y = Inf, label = "ORF body", size = 3, color = "grey40", vjust = 2) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = -3, xmax = 3, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
             fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.2) +
    labs(title = "L. Start Site SHAP Importance by NMD Subgroup (±50bp around ATG)",
         subtitle = "Total A+C+G+T |SHAP| per position. Shaded region = Kozak window. PTC- ref ATG retained shows diffuse rather than focal importance.",
         x = "Position relative to ATG", y = "Total nucleotide |SHAP|", color = NULL) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

  save_fig(p_shap_start, "fig6_shap_start_site", width = 10, height = 6)
}
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** **PTC+** shows a sharp, focal SHAP peak centered precisely on the ATG (position 0), reflecting the model's strong reliance on the canonical Kozak context at the reference CDS start codon.
- **(Data)** **PTC- ref ATG retained** shows a broader, more diffuse SHAP distribution across the ±50bp window. Rather than concentrating importance at the ATG itself, the model distributes importance into the flanking 5'UTR — consistent with detecting upstream ORFs or translational context features that explain non-canonical NMD.
- **(Interpretation)** This visualization complements the attention analysis (Panels J-K): for PTC- ref ATG retained, both attention and SHAP spread away from the rank-0 ORF's start site, suggesting the model learns that the reference ATG is not the key feature and instead searches the broader sequence context.

## 6.9 Attention Entropy Correlates with Subgroup and Model Confidence {#sec6-entropy}

```{r fig6e-entropy-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: sec8_data (attention entropy + subgroup assignments)
# Methods: METHODS.md § "Attention Analysis > Attention entropy"
# Population: all NMD test transcripts (n = n_nmd_sec8)

# Panel A: Entropy distribution by subgroup
entropy_n <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  filter(!is.na(attn_entropy)) %>%
  count(subgroup)
entropy_labels <- setNames(
  sprintf("%s\n(n=%s)", entropy_n$subgroup, format(entropy_n$n, big.mark = ",")),
  entropy_n$subgroup)
nmd_entropy_plot <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  filter(!is.na(attn_entropy)) %>%
  mutate(subgroup_label = entropy_labels[as.character(subgroup)],
         subgroup_label = factor(subgroup_label,
           levels = entropy_labels[c("NMD PTC+", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained", "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost")]))

n_entropy <- nrow(nmd_entropy_plot)

p_ent_sub <- nmd_entropy_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup_label, y = attn_entropy, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("M. Attention Entropy by NMD Subgroup (n=%s)", format(n_entropy, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "PTC- and ref-ATG-lost subgroups have higher entropy (more distributed attention across ORFs).",
       x = NULL, y = "Attention entropy (bits)") +
  guides(fill = "none")

# Panel B: Accuracy by entropy quartile (within NMD)
nmd_with_entropy <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  filter(!is.na(attn_entropy)) %>%
  mutate(correct = outcome == "TP",
         entropy_quartile = cut(attn_entropy,
                                breaks = quantile(attn_entropy, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
                                labels = c("Q1 (low)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (high)"),
                                include.lowest = TRUE))

q_summary <- nmd_with_entropy %>%
  filter(!is.na(entropy_quartile)) %>%
  group_by(entropy_quartile) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), accuracy = mean(correct),
            mean_entropy = mean(attn_entropy, na.rm = TRUE),
            pct_ref_cds = round(100 * mean(is_ref_cds, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
            pct_ejc = round(100 * mean(has_downstream_ejc, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
            mean_upstream_atgs = mean(n_upstream_atgs, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

p_acc <- q_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = entropy_quartile, y = accuracy)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#ef8a62", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%\nn=%d", 100*accuracy, n)),
            vjust = -0.2, size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title = sprintf("N. Prediction Accuracy by Attention Entropy Quartile (n=%s NMD)", format(n_entropy, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Low entropy (one dominant ORF) correlates with high accuracy; high entropy with more errors.",
       x = "Attention entropy quartile", y = "Accuracy (% correctly predicted as NMD)")

save_fig(p_ent_sub / p_acc, "fig6e_entropy_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 8)
```

<details><summary>Table 6d: Entropy quartile summary</summary>
```{r table7c-entropy}
render_dt(q_summary %>%
            mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3))),
          caption = "Table 6d: NMD transcripts by attention entropy quartile")
```
</details>

**Variable definitions:**

- **Attention entropy:** Shannon entropy (bits) of the attention weight vector across the 5 candidate ORFs. 0 bits = all attention on one ORF. Higher values indicate the model distributes attention across multiple ORFs.

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** NMD PTC- and NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost subgroups have higher attention entropy than NMD PTC+, indicating the model is less certain which ORF to focus on for non-canonical transcripts.
- **(Data)** Accuracy declines monotonically with entropy: Q1 (`r round(100*q_summary$accuracy[q_summary$entropy_quartile == "Q1 (low)"], 1)`%) to Q4 (`r round(100*q_summary$accuracy[q_summary$entropy_quartile == "Q4 (high)"], 1)`%). This is consistent with the subgroup analysis — the high-entropy quartile is enriched for PTC- and ref-ATG-lost transcripts.

## 6.10 Joint DeepSHAP Across All 5 ORFs {#sec6-all-orfs}

```{r fig6-all-orfs-setup}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: deepshap_all_orfs_summary_{tag}.tsv — per-sample per-ORF total |SHAP|
# Produced by aggregating deepshap_joint_{tag}[_orf{1-4}]_run1.npz files
# Population: all test transcripts, 500 background, joint DeepSHAP run 1

all_orfs_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("deepshap_all_orfs_summary_", best_tag, ".tsv"))
has_all_orfs <- file.exists(all_orfs_path)
```

```{r fig6-all-orfs, eval=has_all_orfs, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
all_orfs <- read.delim(all_orfs_path)

# Join with subgroup assignments
all_orfs_sg <- all_orfs %>%
  left_join(sec8_data %>% select(isoform_id, subgroup),
            by = "isoform_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(subgroup))

# Panel O: Mean total |SHAP| by ORF rank (NMD vs Control)
orf_by_class <- all_orfs %>%
  mutate(class = ifelse(label == 1, "NMD", "Control")) %>%
  group_by(orf_rank, class) %>%
  summarise(mean_shap = mean(total_abs_shap),
            se = sd(total_abs_shap) / sqrt(n()),
            n = n(), .groups = "drop")

p_orf_overall <- orf_by_class %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(orf_rank), y = mean_shap, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_shap - se, ymax = mean_shap + se),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), width = 0.15) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", mean_shap)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), vjust = -0.8, size = 2.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_y_sqrt(breaks = c(0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25)) +
  labs(title = sprintf("O. Joint DeepSHAP: Mean Total SHAP Magnitude by ORF Rank (n=%s transcripts)",
                       format(length(unique(all_orfs$isoform_id)), big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "ORF 0 (rank-0, priority CDS) dominates; secondary ORFs contribute modestly. Y-axis: square-root scale.",
       x = "ORF rank", y = expression("Mean total" ~ group("|", SHAP, "|")), fill = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Panel P: Branch decomposition by ORF rank (NMD only)
branch_by_orf <- all_orfs %>%
  filter(label == 1) %>%
  group_by(orf_rank) %>%
  summarise(ATG = mean(atg_abs_shap),
            Stop = mean(stop_abs_shap),
            Structural = mean(struct_abs_shap),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ATG, Stop, Structural), names_to = "branch", values_to = "mean_shap") %>%
  mutate(branch = factor(branch, levels = c("Structural", "Stop", "ATG")))

p_branch_orf <- branch_by_orf %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(orf_rank), y = mean_shap, fill = branch)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Structural" = "#d95f02", "Stop" = "#FF6B6B", "ATG" = "#4ECDC4")) +
  labs(title = "P. Branch Decomposition by ORF Rank (NMD transcripts)",
       subtitle = "ORF 0 drives prediction through all three branches; secondary ORFs contribute primarily through sequence.",
       x = "ORF rank", y = "Mean total |SHAP|", fill = NULL)

# Panel Q: Per-ORF |SHAP| by subgroup (NMD only)
orf_by_sg <- all_orfs_sg %>%
  filter(label == 1) %>%
  group_by(orf_rank, subgroup) %>%
  summarise(mean_shap = mean(total_abs_shap), .groups = "drop")

p_orf_sg <- orf_by_sg %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(orf_rank), y = mean_shap, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal_short) +
  labs(title = "Q. Joint DeepSHAP by ORF Rank and NMD Subgroup",
       subtitle = "PTC+ concentrates importance on ORF 0; PTC- subgroups distribute more to secondary ORFs.",
       x = "ORF rank", y = "Mean total |SHAP|", fill = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

save_fig(p_orf_overall / p_branch_orf / p_orf_sg,
         "fig6_all_orfs_shap", width = 10, height = 14)
```

```{r tbl6-all-orfs, eval=has_all_orfs}
# Summary table: fraction of total |SHAP| by ORF rank
orf_frac <- all_orfs_sg %>%
  filter(label == 1) %>%
  group_by(isoform_id, subgroup) %>%
  mutate(total_across_orfs = sum(total_abs_shap),
         frac = total_abs_shap / total_across_orfs) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(subgroup, orf_rank) %>%
  summarise(mean_frac = round(100 * mean(frac), 1),
            mean_abs_shap = round(mean(total_abs_shap), 2),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = orf_rank, values_from = c(mean_frac, mean_abs_shap),
              names_glue = "orf{orf_rank}_{.value}")

render_dt(orf_frac, caption = "Table 6e: Fraction of total |SHAP| by ORF rank and subgroup (NMD only)")
```

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** ORF 0 (the priority CDS ORF) accounts for the vast majority of total |SHAP| across all subgroups. Secondary ORFs (ranks 1–4) contribute modestly, with diminishing importance at higher ranks.
- **(Data)** The NMD vs Control gap is concentrated in ORF 0: NMD transcripts show substantially higher |SHAP| at rank 0, while secondary ORFs show similar |SHAP| between classes.
- **(Data)** PTC- subgroups show relatively higher fractional contribution from secondary ORFs compared to PTC+, consistent with the higher attention entropy and more distributed prediction strategy observed in Sections 6.8–6.9.

## 6.11 Summary {#sec6-summary}

The subgroup analyses in this section converge on a consistent picture. NMD transcripts that lack the canonical PTC + EJC signature fall into two structurally distinct subgroups — **PTC-, ref ATG retained** (ref reading frame terminates without downstream EJCs) and **PTC-, ref ATG lost** (ref ATG unavailable due to alternative TSS or structural differences):

1. **Structural distinctiveness (Section 6.2):** Both PTC- subgroups have longer 5'UTRs and higher 5'UTR ORF coverage than both NMD PTC+ and Control transcripts. NMD PTC+ and Control are indistinguishable on these features — the 5'UTR complexity signal is specific to non-canonical NMD.

2. **Different sequence signals (Sections 6.4–6.5):** DeepSHAP motif logos by subgroup reveal fundamentally different start and stop codon context patterns. PTC+ shows strong Kozak signals at the start codon and dominant junction/TGA signals at the stop. PTC- ref ATG retained shows weak or absent signals at both sites.

3. **Different ORF feature reliance (Section 6.7):** Attribution analysis shows PTC+ relies on `n_downstream_ejc` and `is_ref_cds`, with diminished importance for PTC- subgroups where the canonical EJC signature is absent.

4. **Qualitatively different branch strategies (Section 6.7):** KernelSHAP branch decomposition reveals the ATG (start codon) branch rises from 8% of total attribution in PTC+ to **27% in PTC- ref ATG retained**, dominating 25% of those isoforms. The model shifts prediction strategy from EJC-dependent (structural + stop branch) to start-codon-dependent for non-canonical NMD.

5. **Higher attention uncertainty (Sections 6.8–6.9):** Both PTC- subgroups have higher attention entropy and lower accuracy, reflecting ambiguous ORF landscapes.

6. **Reduced model performance (Section 7.2):** The model achieves `r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"], 1)`% sensitivity on PTC+ but only `r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"], 1)`% on PTC- ref ATG retained and `r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"], 1)`% on PTC- ref ATG lost, with predictions clustering near the decision boundary.

**Implications:**

- **(Hypothesis)** The NMD PTC- subpopulations may represent alternative NMD pathways — including uORF-driven translational dysregulation or mechanisms other than the classical EJC model **(literature: Metze et al., 2013; Karousis & Muhlemann, 2019)**. The model's shift to the ATG branch for PTC- ref ATG retained isoforms suggests it has learned to detect 5'UTR-based NMD signals that may reflect these alternative mechanisms.
- **(Implication for model improvement)** The attention heterogeneity and reduced performance on PTC- subgroups suggest the current architecture's single-ORF-dominant assumption is suboptimal for non-canonical NMD. A cross-attention mechanism between ORF sequence branches and structural features could improve performance on ambiguous ORF landscapes.


---

# 7. Model Performance and Error Analysis {#sec7-performance}

---

## 7.1 Model Error Analysis {#sec7-errors}

**Question:** What isoforms does the model fail to predict?

**Population:** `r format(n_test, big.mark = ",")` test transcripts at threshold = 0.5.

```{r sec7-classify}
# outcome already computed in Section 0 data loading
n_tp <- sum(preds$outcome == "TP"); n_fn <- sum(preds$outcome == "FN")
n_fp <- sum(preds$outcome == "FP"); n_tn <- sum(preds$outcome == "TN")
sensitivity <- round(100 * n_tp / (n_tp + n_fn), 1)
specificity <- round(100 * n_tn / (n_tn + n_fp), 1)
```

**Confusion matrix (threshold = 0.5):** TP = `r n_tp`, FN = `r n_fn`, FP = `r n_fp`, TN = `r n_tn`. Sensitivity = `r sensitivity`%, Specificity = `r specificity`%.

```{r fig7a-calibration}
cal_data <- preds %>%
  mutate(prob_bin = cut(prob, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), include.lowest = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(prob_bin) %>%
  summarise(mean_pred = mean(prob), obs_rate = mean(label), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

p_cal <- cal_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_pred, y = obs_rate)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
```
