```{r fig7a-calibration}
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "n transcripts") +
  labs(title = sprintf("A. Calibration Plot (n=%s test transcripts)", format(n_test, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Predicted probability vs observed NMD rate in decile bins. Point size = bin count.",
       x = "Mean predicted P(NMD)", y = "Observed NMD rate") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1))

save_fig(p_cal, "fig7a_calibration")
```

```{r fig7b-features-by-outcome, fig.height=8}
outcome_features <- test_rank0 %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, outcome), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  select(isoform_id, outcome, has_downstream_ejc, is_ref_cds,
         n_downstream_ejc, orf_length) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(isoform_id, outcome), names_to = "feature", values_to = "value")

p_feat <- outcome_features %>%
  filter(feature %in% c("has_downstream_ejc", "is_ref_cds",
                          "n_downstream_ejc", "orf_length")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = outcome, y = value, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.7, scale = "width") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, fill = "white", alpha = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~feature, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(TP = "#ef8a62", FN = "#d95f02",
                                FP = "#67a9cf", TN = "#2166ac")) +
  labs(title = "B. Key Features by Prediction Outcome",
       subtitle = "TP/FN = NMD transcripts. FP/TN = Control transcripts.",
       x = NULL, y = "Feature value") +
  guides(fill = "none")

save_fig(p_feat, "fig7b_features_by_outcome", height = 8)
```

**Variable definitions:**

- **TP:** NMD correctly predicted (prob > 0.5). **FN:** NMD missed. **FP:** Control falsely flagged. **TN:** Control correct.
- **Calibration plot:** Perfect calibration follows the diagonal. Point size = bin count.

<details><summary>Table 7a: False Negative characterization</summary>
```{r table7a-fn}
fn_char <- test_rank0 %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, outcome), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  filter(outcome == "FN") %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            pct_ref_cds = round(100 * mean(is_ref_cds == 1), 1),
            pct_downstream_ejc = round(100 * mean(has_downstream_ejc == 1), 1),
            median_prob = round(median(prob), 3),
            mean_orf_length = round(mean(orf_length)))
render_dt(fn_char, caption = "Table 7a: False negatives (NMD predicted as non-NMD)")
```
</details>

<details><summary>Table 7b: False Positive characterization</summary>
```{r table7b-fp}
fp_char <- test_rank0 %>%
  left_join(preds %>% select(isoform_id, outcome), by = "isoform_id") %>%
  filter(outcome == "FP") %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            pct_ref_cds = round(100 * mean(is_ref_cds == 1), 1),
            pct_downstream_ejc = round(100 * mean(has_downstream_ejc == 1), 1),
            median_prob = round(median(prob), 3),
            mean_orf_length = round(mean(orf_length)))
render_dt(fp_char, caption = "Table 7b: False positives (non-NMD predicted as NMD)")
```
</details>

```{r sec7-noptc, include=FALSE}
n_noptc <- nrow(noptc)
noptc_pct <- round(100 * n_noptc / n_nmd, 1)
noptc_correct <- sum(noptc$prob > 0.5)
noptc_mean_prob <- round(mean(noptc$prob), 3)
```

**No-PTC NMD isoforms (strict definition):** `r n_noptc` of `r format(n_nmd, big.mark = ",")` NMD transcripts (`r noptc_pct`%) have no ORF among the 5 candidates with any downstream EJC. The model correctly predicts only `r noptc_correct` of `r n_noptc` as NMD (mean P(NMD) = `r noptc_mean_prob`).

**Broader non-canonical NMD population:** The `r n_noptc` strict no-PTC cases are the extreme tail. A much larger population of NMD transcripts has ambiguous or absent PTC evidence: `r sum(preds$outcome == "FN")` false negatives (Section 7.2), the highest-entropy attention quartile (Section 6.9), and isoforms where the reference ATG is lost or the ref CDS has few downstream EJCs. These likely represent a continuum of non-canonical NMD pathways — including long-3'UTR-mediated NMD (Metze et al., 2013), translational readthrough, and alternative ORF engagement (Karousis & Muhlemann, 2019). See Section 6 for detailed subgroup characterization.

**Key observations:** All feature values below are for the **rank-0 (priority) ORF** per transcript, not transcript-level aggregates.

- **(Data)** False negatives have lower downstream EJC rates (`r fn_char$pct_downstream_ejc`% vs `r round(100 * mean(test_rank0$has_downstream_ejc[test_rank0$label == 1], na.rm = TRUE), 1)`% for all NMD) — the model fails when the PTC signature is absent.
- **(Data)** False positives have elevated downstream EJC rates (`r fp_char$pct_downstream_ejc`% vs `r round(100 * mean(test_rank0$has_downstream_ejc[test_rank0$label == 0], na.rm = TRUE), 1)`% for all Control) — these are structurally NMD-like controls.
- **(Data)** The `r nrow(noptc)` no-PTC NMD isoforms represent a fundamental limitation — the primary predictive feature (downstream EJC) is absent, and the model relies on weaker signals (mean P(NMD) = `r noptc_mean_prob`).

## 7.2 Model Performance Differs by Subgroup {#sec7-performance-subgroup}

```{r fig7c-performance-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: sec8_data (predictions + subgroup assignments)
# Population: all NMD test transcripts (n = n_nmd), plus Control for specificity context

nmd_subgroups <- sec8_data %>% filter(label == 1)
n_nmd_sec8 <- nrow(nmd_subgroups)

perf_by_subgroup <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            n_correct = sum(outcome == "TP"),
            sensitivity = mean(outcome == "TP"),
            mean_prob = mean(prob),
            median_prob = median(prob),
            mean_entropy = mean(attn_entropy, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

# Panel A: Sensitivity by subgroup
p_sens <- perf_by_subgroup %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = sensitivity, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%\n%d/%d", 100*sensitivity, n_correct, n)),
            vjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1.1)) +
  labs(title = sprintf("C. Sensitivity by Subgroup (n=%s NMD transcripts)", format(n_nmd_sec8, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "The model performs well on PTC+ but substantially worse on PTC- and ref-ATG-lost subgroups.",
       x = NULL, y = "Sensitivity (% correctly predicted as NMD)") +
  guides(fill = "none")

# Panel B: P(NMD) distribution by subgroup
p_prob <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = prob, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  labs(title = sprintf("D. Predicted P(NMD) Distribution by Subgroup (n=%s)", format(n_nmd_sec8, big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "Dashed line = classification threshold. PTC- and ref-ATG-lost groups cluster near the boundary.",
       x = NULL, y = "Predicted P(NMD)") +
  guides(fill = "none")

save_fig(p_sens / p_prob, "fig7c_performance_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 8)
```

```{r fig7d-auc-auprc-by-subgroup, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# ── Data provenance ──
# Source: sec8_data (predictions + subgroup assignments)
# Population: each NMD subgroup vs all Control transcripts (one-vs-rest)

ctrl_data <- sec8_data %>% filter(label == 0)

subgroup_metrics <- nmd_subgroups %>%
  group_by(subgroup) %>%
  group_modify(function(sg_df, key) {
    combined <- bind_rows(sg_df, ctrl_data)
    roc_obj <- pROC::roc(combined$label, combined$prob, levels = c(0, 1), quiet = TRUE)
    auc_val <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj))
    # AUPRC via trapezoidal integration of precision-recall curve
    pr <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, x = "all", ret = c("threshold", "recall", "precision"),
                       transpose = FALSE)
    pr <- pr %>% filter(!is.na(precision) & !is.na(recall)) %>% arrange(recall)
    auprc_val <- sum(diff(pr$recall) * (pr$precision[-1] + pr$precision[-nrow(pr)]) / 2)
    tibble(auc = auc_val, auprc = auprc_val, n = nrow(sg_df))
  }) %>% ungroup()

metrics_long <- subgroup_metrics %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(auc, auprc), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = toupper(metric))

p_metrics_sg <- metrics_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = subgroup, y = value, fill = subgroup)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f\n(n=%d)", value, n)),
            vjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subgroup_pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.15)) +
  labs(title = sprintf("E. AUC and AUPRC by Isoform Subtype (each vs %s Controls)",
                       format(nrow(ctrl_data), big.mark = ",")),
       subtitle = "One-vs-rest: each NMD subgroup evaluated against all Control transcripts.",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

save_fig(p_metrics_sg, "fig7d_auc_auprc_by_subgroup", width = 10, height = 5)
```

<details><summary>Table 7d: AUC/AUPRC by subgroup</summary>
```{r table7d-metrics}
render_dt(subgroup_metrics %>%
            mutate(across(c(auc, auprc), ~round(., 4))),
          caption = "Table 7d: AUC and AUPRC by NMD subgroup (one-vs-rest)")
```
</details>

<details><summary>Table 7c: Performance by subgroup</summary>
```{r table7b-performance}
render_dt(perf_by_subgroup %>%
            mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3))),
          caption = "Table 7c: Model performance by NMD subgroup")
```
</details>

**Observations:**

- **(Data)** NMD PTC+ sensitivity (`r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC+"], 1)`%) is substantially higher than PTC-, ref ATG retained (`r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG retained"], 1)`%) and PTC-, ref ATG lost (`r round(100*perf_by_subgroup$sensitivity[perf_by_subgroup$subgroup == "NMD PTC-, ref ATG lost"], 1)`%).
- **(Data)** Both PTC- subgroups have lower mean P(NMD) and more predictions near the decision boundary, consistent with the model having weaker evidence for non-canonical NMD transcripts.
- **(Data)** AUC and AUPRC (one-vs-rest against Controls) confirm the subgroup hierarchy: PTC+ has the highest discriminative performance, while PTC- ref ATG retained and ref ATG lost show substantially lower AUPRC, reflecting the difficulty of identifying non-canonical NMD transcripts.

# Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```

---

*Report generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)*
